Tenders.Net #: 1602379
Agency: Australian Electoral Commission

Closing: 24/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (Canberra Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*

Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: LH-05462 - 1 Manager Project Manager
The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) is looking to engage an enthusiastic Project Manager to join our IT Infrastructure team, supporting the delivery and sustainment of the AEC’s Cyber security objectives. The Project Manager’s role is to actively develop and manage a work program including sprint schedules, backlogs and reporting the delivery and sustainment of the AEC’s Cyber security objectives. This will be achieved through working with a dedicated team and technical SME’s across the Division. The role will also be the primary liaison point with the Integrity branch including Cyber and physical security teams and other business stakeholders. The Chief Technology and Data Officer Branch deliver a range of ICT services to the agency including infrastructure, application development and digital communications. ICT services are delivered through a select sourcing model, with a mixture of in-house and outsourced resources.
The candidate must hold an active Baseline security clearance prior to commencement.
Key duties and responsibilities
The successful Project Manager will be responsible for, but not limited to;
• Work plan governance and reporting.
• Work with the project’s Program Manager and technical lead to define and manage the delivery of work packages utilising Scrum and Azure DevOps.
• Identify and manage project risks and issues;
• Lead workshops with internal stakeholders and actively deliver and review analysis artefacts (Azure DevOps Epics, Features and User Stories);
• Implement effective change control;
• Coordinate AEC staff to meet the delivery timeframes.
• Support contract managers in managing vendors to deliver outcomes;
• Liaise with business and technical areas to facilitate the timely resolution of issues as they arise;
• Produce technical and non-technical documentation as required;
• Manage project resources effectively to deliver the required outcomes.

Details: LH-05954 - 1 System Support Analyst
The AEC is seeking to engage a System Support Analyst to support the Information management Team.
The candidate must hold an active Baseline security clearance prior to commencement.
Key duties and responsibilities
Role will be responsible for, but not limited to:
• Conduct Objective system administration and helpdesk tasks to support the implementation and ongoing maintenance of the electronic document and records management system (EDRMS);
• Provide accurate advice, guidance, and training on recordkeeping
• within Objective;
• Develop tools and resources to support the implementation and ongoing maintenance of Objective;
• Conduct quality assurance activities to ensure usage is compliant with Information management standards and policies;
• Develop and support internal and external relationships, including troubleshooting and working with internal areas on system changes to support improved alignment of business processes and Objective usage;
• Analyse and collate statistical information and develop reports for stakeholders in relation to records management and Objective usage;
• Make and communicate decisions using good judgement, expertise and knowledge, governed by legislation, regulations, best practice principles or relevant operating instructions and procedures;
• Contribute to the management of paper records including advice, and digitisation of records in accordance with the associated legislation;
• Create and maintain records and information in accordance with the Archives Act 1983;
• Participating in regular performance management discussions with their relevant Director/Team Leader; and
• Perform additional duties or assume responsibility of functions as directed from time to time.

Deadline for asking questions
Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-05673 - 1 Product Manager
Job details
The HPRG Digital Core Products – Product Manager role is responsible for end-to-end leadership, strategic direction, and delivery of HPRG’s data and service management products. The role ensures that core digital services and data platforms are designed, delivered, and evolved in a way that supports regulatory outcomes, enables business efficiency, and meets internal and external user needs.
Key duties and responsibilities
The Product Manager will be responsible for:
• Leading a multidisciplinary product team using agile and user‑centred digital delivery methods.
• Working with the Product Owner and Executive to shape priorities and product direction.
• Driving delivery against agreed program and product roadmaps, managing a prioritised and maturing backlog to meet user needs.
• Actively managing and escalating risks, issues, and dependencies impacting delivery.
• Collaborating across the HPRG Digital Branch and with key stakeholders, including business areas, ITD, and regulatory partners.
• Communicating delivery progress, risks, and priorities clearly and consistently to senior leadership and delivery teams.
With key capabilities in:
• Demonstrated agile product management skills and experience.
• Demonstrated ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and manage complex stakeholder environments.
• Demonstrated knowledge and application of government processes, including (but not limited to) budget processes and ministerial and executive briefings.
• Strong understanding of digital service standards, user‑centred design, and service transformation.
• Ability to work accurately in a fast‑paced environment, applying strong organisational, interpersonal, and problem‑solving skills to navigate competing priorities.
• Sound judgement, with the ability to escalate issues appropriately.
• Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, including the ability to build and maintain productive working relationships.
• Strong leadership and team management skills.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to translate complex technical information for non‑technical audiences.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Monday, 20 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: PCS-03818 - Human Centred Design Exploration: Investors in Australian Critical Minerals Opportunities
Requirements
Geoscience Australia is seeking a Human Centred Design (HCD) team to work with our Minerals, Energy and Groundwater Division and with our Digital Experience team to create an actionable understanding of current and potential financial investors in the Australian critical mineral sector, including their motivations and information needs.
This body of work will contribute to defining digital products related to critical minerals and inform Geoscience Australia’s HCD digital service experience strategy.
The results of this work will be a typology of financial investors, and their information needs that will provide insight on:
WHAT: Current state and size of the investment ecosystem and stakeholders in financial investment in the Australian critical mineral sector including relevant geo-political, macro-economic, and technological factors that determine and shape investment. This ecosystem should include the tripartite approach of raising funds by mining-government investment mechanisms and equipment manufacturers.
HOW: Common investment processes, decision points, risk considerations, and key milestones that shape financial involvement in Australian critical mineral opportunities as they move from early, speculative exploration through to mature, commercially viable resource projects.
WHO: Types and proportions of investors in this ecosystem, and when, how, and with what criteria and information different types of investors choose to invest (or divest) in opportunities across the development pipeline. This ecosystem involves banks, equity firms, private capital, major mining companies, stockbrokers, and government finance agencies.
WHY: Understanding the drivers of investor behaviour and the criteria and information used by specific investors at each stage of development
GAP: What data or information could improve and promote confident decision-making in Australian critical minerals projects, but are currently unavailable, difficult to source, or otherwise challenging to incorporate and/or communicate? What role do investors see for Geoscience Australia in providing such data or information?
Activities:
• 1 Kick off session with project sponsors
• Conduct user experience research including qualitative and quantitative methods suitable to addressing the project goals, such as through interviews, qualitative surveys, workshops and desktop research.
• Creation and delivery of research data documents such as transcripts, workshop reports, bibliographies from desktop research
• Data analysis and insights generation
• Create typology artefacts (see deliverables below)
• 2 sessions in which draft analysis and artifacts are shared with project sponsors
• 2 sessions presenting final analysis and materials to project sponsors
Artifacts and deliverables:
• Qualitative and Quantitative Research data documents including transcripts, session notes, workshop reports, bibliographies and the like.
• Insights report that pulls together and provides overarching narrative and context for typology artifacts and identifies challenges in data availability, sourcing, formatting, etc.
• that if addressed could provide more certainty related to investments.
• Current state ecosystem and stakeholder maps of financial investment in the Australian critical mineral sector
• Australian critical mineral project development journey maps for archetypal mineral resources, characterising common financial investment activities and decisions from pre-competitive geoscience data creation and delivery, through to mature commercially viable projects
• Investor Archetypes including when, how, and with what criteria and information they choose to invest (or divest) in opportunities across the development pipeline
• Investor Archetypes journey maps that detail investment decision milestones, the criteria and information that is used, that is available (but not used), that is not available (and desired), across the development pipeline.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Deadline for asking questions
Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06063 - 1 Enterprise Architect
Job details
Enterprise Architects create and evolve architecture models that inform the design, development and evolution of business processes and supporting ICT systems.
Key duties and responsibilities
Enterprise Architecture Leadership
• Provide senior, specialist enterprise architecture leadership across NEMA’s complex and mission‑critical ICT environment, supporting national emergency management, disaster response, recovery, and communications capabilities.
• Establish and maintain a coherent enterprise architecture that ensures NEMA’s digital platforms are scalable, secure, resilient, and aligned with the Agency’s evolving business needs and operating model.
Architectural Oversight and Assurance
• Deliver architectural oversight and assurance across NEMA’s ICT portfolio, ensuring new and enhanced systems align with enterprise architecture standards, governance arrangements, and Whole‑of‑Government digital and architectural guidance.
• Review and assure solution designs for new and emerging technology‑enabled initiatives, identifying and managing technical dependencies, risks, and enterprise‑wide impacts.
• Ensure architectural decisions support long‑term sustainability, interoperability, and value for money.
Strategic Advisory and Support
• Provide expert architectural advice to inform ICT investment decisions, enabling a clear understanding of enterprise impacts, and long‑term outcomes.
• Support prioritisation and sequencing of concurrent initiatives by maintaining a holistic view of the enterprise landscape and architectural roadmap.
• Translate complex business requirements into coherent, end‑to‑end architectural designs that support NEMA’s strategic and operational objectives.
Delivery Enablement
• Work closely with ICT teams to embed architectural discipline throughout design and delivery phases.
• Support ongoing system enhancement activities by ensuring architectural consistency and alignment across successive delivery stages.
• Proactively identify and resolve architectural issues that could impact schedule, cost, risk, or operational outcomes.
Knowledge management and Transfer
• Capture, document, and transfer critical architectural knowledge from design and delivery phases to reduce delivery and operational risk.
• Produce, maintain, and govern architectural artefacts, models, standards, and decision records to support organisational maturity and future capability uplift.
• Provide specialist enterprise architecture expertise at the required scale and depth where this capability is not available in‑house.

Details: LH-06233 - 1 Program Manager
Job details
The Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF) is seeking an experienced Program Manager or senior Project Manager to lead the delivery of ICT Networks projects within the ICT services Division (ISD).
ISD is responsible for managing and delivering Information and Communications Technology (ICT) functions across Australia and internationally, ensuring a secure, modern ICT environment that enables the department to operate 24/7 and achieve its strategic objectives.
As part of the Capital security Technology and asset Refresh (CapSTAR) Program, ISD requires a Program Manager to oversee the Network Edge sub-stream and its associated projects. This role will focus on refreshing ageing network infrastructure by implementing supportable devices and modernised network capabilities. Key objectives include enhancing network management and monitoring, as well as expanding Wi-Fi coverage across DAFF sites to improve connectivity for staff.
Key duties and responsibilities
The successful candidate will be responsible for:
Governance & assurance – Ensure delivery aligns with ICT governance, policies, regulatory requirements, and stage gate approvals across network and edge programs.
Stakeholder alignment – Engage business, product, security, and technical stakeholders to define and prioritise requirements aligned to network strategy and cyber security objectives.
Executive reporting – Produce concise, high-quality reporting and governance artefacts with clear visibility of progress, risks, dependencies, and decisions.
Technical coordination – Lead cross-functional coordination across engineering, architecture, infrastructure, security, and vendors to align design, integration, and delivery.
Financial management – Own budget end-to-end, including forecasting, cost control, and variance management; ensure delivery within approved funding and drive cost optimisation and commercial discipline.
Integrated delivery control – Maintain oversight of schedule, budget, risks, issues, and dependencies, ensuring alignment to baselines across multi-site deployments.
Change & risk management – Govern change and proactively manage risks and issues, ensuring impacts to design, security, budget, and delivery are assessed and controlled.
Delivery planning – Drive integrated plans across network, security, and infrastructure, actively managing dependencies such as carriage, site readiness, and vendor inputs.
Vendor management – Manage vendors and partners to deliver against scope, service levels, quality, timeframes, and commercial obligations.
Operational readiness – Ensure effective transition to operations, including service acceptance, documentation, and support readiness.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: RFT 03/26-03 - Balranald Ben Scott Memorial Trail
The Ben Scott Memorial Bird Trail, a key component of the Tri-Avian Corridor and a major tourism asset for the Balranald region, sustained significant damage during the AGRN 1034 flood event in November 2022. The Ben Scott Memorial Bird Trail Remediation Project seeks to restore, refresh, and expand this facility. This project will be delivered via a Design and Construct (D&C) approach to ensure the integration of flood-resilient infrastructure with the existing natural landscape.

Engineering services - prototype biosecurity boot-hygiene stations

Tenders.Net #: 1602344
Agency: Lord Howe Island Board
Location: NSW
Closing: 30/04/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Darryl Birch
Team Leader Biosecurity
E: darryl.birch@lhib.nsw.gov.au

Krissy Ward
Senior Environment Manager
E: kristine.ward@lhib.nsw.gov.au
P: 02 6563 2066

Darryl Birch, Team Leader Biosecurity, is available to discuss the proposed design parameters and arrange a field visit to the stations in situ. This will occur on receipt of the submissions.

Email your submission to:
Administration Office
E: administration@lhib.nsw.gov.au
Use the subject line: RFQ – Engineering Services: Design and construction of prototype Biosecurity boot-hygiene stations. Attention: Darryl Birch

The Lord Howe Island Board is not bound to accept the lowest-priced or any submission.
LHIB reserves the right to discontinue the process or seek further information from respondents at any time.
Details: Householder – Request for quotation — Engineering services: Design and construction of prototype Biosecurity boot-hygiene stations
The Lord Howe Island Board (LHIB) are seeking quotations from skilled local contractors to modify two existing boot-hygiene stations. These modified stations will serve as prototypes for a proposed upgrade to all the stations on the island.
Boot-hygiene stations are a key part of the Board’s biosecurity program. They help control the spread of potentially harmful pathogens across the island. They must be effective, easy to use and durable to ensure a high level of compliance by users.
Scope of Works
The required services include, but are not limited to:
• Designing modifications to one large station and one small station (see Figures 1-4 via the link provided).
• Consultation with LHIB during the design stage to ensure the proposed modifications meet functional and operational biosecurity requirements.
• Fabrication primarily with marine grade aluminium/ stainless steel and other materials if required.
• Installation of the stations for a trial period.
• Further modification of the prototype if required.
Project Timeline
LHIB anticipates that the prototype stations will be designed, fabricated and installed in 2026. The exact timing will be agreed with the successful contractor following evaluation of submissions.
Evaluation of Submissions
The Board will evaluate submissions against the following measurable criteria, which must be addressed in the submission:
• Design quality and suitability
Demonstrated alignment of the proposed design approach with functional, operational, and biosecurity requirements, for one large and one small boot hygiene station.
• Engineering and fabrication experience
Evidence of relevant engineering and aluminium fabrication experience.
• Value for money
Demonstrated cost effectiveness of the quoted price for the full scope of works (design and construct) compared with other conforming submissions.
• Licences and insurance
Provision of current and valid evidence of a relevant business licence, public liability and workers compensation insurances.
• Delivery timeframe
Likelihood of delivery within the agreed timeframe, assessed by a detailed project schedule with key milestones to meet the required completion date - end 2026.
Work Health and Safety:
The contractor must comply with all applicable work health and safety legislation and industry standards. The contractor must comply with LHIB site-specific safety requirements and relevant LHIB Job Safety Analyses where applicable to the works.
Approval of Works
On completion of the works, the contractor must notify LHIB and provide evidence that the work is complete. Payment will follow confirmation that the works have been completed in accordance with the agreed scope.
Any design concepts, drawings or prototype modifications developed as part of this engagement will become the intellectual property of the Lord Howe Island Board.

Raquel Bloom
Regional Manager - Western Sydney
P: +61 04 0026 4678
E: rbloom@icnnsw.org.au
Details: Sydney Metro West - Systems Frameworks Package
Honeywell has been awarded the system framework contract for design, supply, installation and maintenance of below systems for Sydney Metro West project:
• Building management control Systems to supervise operation of the Sydney Metro West stations; and
• Electronic access control Systems to manage staff security access to operational areas of the stations
• Closed Circuit Television Systems (CCTV System) to ensure safe, fast and reliable services,
• Fire Detection Systems to ensure safety across the alignment.
• Public Address Systems to keep customers informed at all times.
• Passenger Information display Systems providing display boards with up-to-date trip information.
Project location and context
The Sydney Metro West project is a new 24-kilometre underground metro railway which will double rail capacity between Parramatta and the Sydney CBD, transforming Sydney for generations to come.
This once-in-a-century infrastructure investment will provide fast, reliable turn-up-and-go metro services with fully accessible stations, link new communities to rail services and support employment growth and housing supply
Stations have been confirmed at Westmead, Parramatta, Sydney Olympic Park, North Strathfield, Burwood North, Five Dock, The Bays, Pyrmont and Hunter Street in the Sydney CBD.
Sydney Metro West will target an opening date of 2032.
===
WORK PACKAGES

Building management control System (BMCS) - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026)
Closed-circuit television (CCTV) system - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026)

Details: MAAS-RFQ 26/007 - Powerhouse Exhibition fit out - Task Eternal - Exhibition Showcase
Description
The Museum of Applied Arts and Sciences (Powerhouse) seeks Requests for Quote (RFQ) for the delivery of exhibition fit out services for the Powerhouse Parramatta opening exhibitions.
Project goals
Powerhouse is producing Task Eternal, the largest exhibition for the opening of Powerhouse Parramatta in late 2026. Powerhouse seeks to engage a suitably qualified supplier or suppliers to undertake design development, supply, manufacture/fabricate fit-out elements to house exhibition collection objects and commissions.
The service requirement includes:
• Pre-production and briefing with Powerhouse’s Technical Production team and Design and Collections team.
• Prototyping/testing.
• Samples, prototype, finishes sign off by Powerhouse at specified hold points.
• Design development.
• Shop Drawings & 2 x Powerhouse review cycles
• Construction, fabrication and/or supply of furniture, fixtures and equipment.
• Supply and installation of fabricated elements.
• Documentation to enable the Powerhouse to deconstruct or remove the elements at the end of the exhibition.
• Submission of final designs for sign off by Powerhouse teams and/or stakeholder(s).
Scope
The scope of works includes the following item:
Exhibition Showcase
The supply and installation of a showcase for an exhibition scene for Task Eternal as per the drawing package at Attachment A, (to be issued following execution of a signed NDA) including:
• supply and installation of an exhibition showcase for an exhibition scene for Task Eternal
• any associated engineering or structural certification
• Shop drawing production & 2 x Powerhouse review cycles.
• In person review by Powerhouse teams for design & technical.
• delivery and installation at Powerhouse Parramatta
• provision of install and deinstall documentation
• adherence to conservation requirements as detailed on the plan
All elements must be delivered by September 2026.

Weir removal

Tenders.Net #: 1602287
Agency: Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development
Location: NSW
Closing: 14/05/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Scott Nichols
E: tenders@dpird.nsw.gov.au

Additional details/instructions
PRE TENDER MEETING:
Date: 29th April 2026
Time: 1pm
Location: Please refer to Appendix A - Site Location

Tenderers are requested to confirm, via email to the Principal’s Contact Person, that they will attend the pre-tender meeting at least 3 Business Days prior to the nominated date.

SCHEME PARTICIPATION:
PLEASE NOTE: Being registered for the Construction Scheme for Works up to $1 Million (SCM0256) is NOT mandatory for this opportunity.

PLEASE NOTE: Being registered for the Engineering and Technical Scheme (SCM4721) is NOT mandatory for this opportunity.
Details: P_2620822 - Buckenbowra Weir Removal
Description
Demolish & appropriately dispose of a failing weir structure from within Buckenbowra River, near Batemans Bay. Works include localised regrading of river sands to tie into surrounding river bed levels.
Project goals
Weir removal will free movement of water and sediments, along with improved fish passage within the Buckenbowra River. Removing the weir will return this section of the Buckenbowra River back to its former condition.
Scope
The weir is approximately 1 metre high and comprises 2 rows of sheet metal piling core, loose rock filling, gabion basket scour protection and a concrete capping. A concrete and aluminium fishway is present on the western side of the structure adjacent to drop boards that can be used to lower water levels in the weir pool prior to initiation of works. An engineering assessment (obtained by the Principal) will determine if weir abutments will need to be removed or can remain and be made safe as part of these works.
Unless abutments are retained, all weir components should be removed and disposed of appropriately. The structure has deteriorated, with scouring on the downstream side of the weir exposing the sheet metal piling and causing loss and deterioration of gabion rock baskets.
Following removal of weir components, sediment present upstream of the weir will need to be regraded to meet downstream river bed levels on a low gradient to prevent erosion of the river bed.
The site is accessible via Quart Pot Road on the eastern side of Buckenbowra River. The western side is not accessible.
An intended process would be to set up erosion and sediment controls, slowly lower weir pool levels by sequential removal of drop boards. Working from the western bank back to the eastern bank to remove weir components, then regrading river bed across the weir location.

Details: WS5616616826 - Provision of 20 Class 7 Vessels Personal watercraft (Jet Skis)
Supply of 20 personal watercraft (PWC) jet skis. This is in compliance with legislative and environmental risk obligations on water safety in NSW.
Project goals
• Ensuring water safety on NSW waterways
• Supporting injury and accident reduction
• Enabling effective safety patrols, incident response, and compliance operation
• Supporting education and enforcement campaigns relating to PWC (jet skis) use
Scope
1.1 Scope
1. The following general specifications should be met:
2. Rider capacity : 2 or 3 person
3. Hull construction material : Fibreglass or other
4. Manufacture year : 2025 or current year, whichever is the more recent
5. Make : Sea-Doo
6. Length : Minimum 3 metres
Engine
7. The following engine specifications should be met:
8. Stroke type : Four stroke
9. Horsepower : Min 200 HP
10. Fuel type : 91 (minimum) Octane Unleaded Petrol
11. Fuel capacity : Min 50 litres – longer range fuel tanks preferred
12. Driving range : Min 50 kilometres at 4000 RPM continual running
13. Fuel consumption : to be provided by Proponent
14. Other : Closed cooling system preferred

Geoff Reardon
Regional Manager - Murray/Riverina NSW
P: +61 402 632 027
E: GReardon@icnnsw.org.au
Details: Pottinger Energy Park - Wind Farm, Solar Farm, and Battery (BESS) Project
Pottinger Renewables Pty Ltd is a partnership between Someva Renewables and AGL Energy. Someva Renewables is a NSW family-owned, Australian based company, who has spent the past two years measuring the wind and solar assets in the Hay and Edward River Local Government Areas, designing the development and consulting with community. AGL operates Australia's largest private electricity generation portfolio, and as the second-oldest listed company in Australia, has a 185-year history of operation.
The Pottinger Energy Park is a GW-scale wind farm, solar farm and battery energy storage system project. It is located in the outer regional areas of NSW, approximately 60km south of Hay and 110km north of Deniliquin in the rural locality of Booroorban. The project is located entirely within the South West Renewable Energy Zone. The associated infrastructure includes:
- Wind turbines and their hardstands
- Solar panels, single-axis trackers and medium voltage power stations
- Battery energy storage systems
- Internal access tracks
- Public road upgrades
- 330 kV/ 33 kV substations
- 330 kV switchyard
- 330 kV overhead transmission line
- 33 kV underground cables
- Permanent Operations and maintenance building
- Temporary construction compounds, concrete batching plants, accommodation
The project is estimated to provide Full Time Equivalent (FTE) employment for up to 900 personnel during construction and 80 personnel during operations. Construction of the project may commence in late 2026 pending the project's success in gaining South West Renewable Energy Zone access Rights through a tender process with EnergyCo. One of the key merit criteria is likely to be centred on Regional Development and Local Supply Chain. As such, we particularly seek the following details within your expressions of interest:
- The extent (percentage) of steel products and components produced or sourced using locally (Australian) milled steel.
- Number of 'learning workers'* you have and/or will likely have by late 2026, including a description of any strategies you have in place here as a company.
- Number of 'underrepresented groups'** you have and/or will likely have by late 2026, including a description of any strategies you have in place here as a company.
No main contractors have been engaged at this stage. It is expected that most of these work packages will be available through the main contractors.
View Project
---

David Norris
Regional Manager - Central West NSW
T: +61 447 800 239
E: dnorris@icnnsw.org.au
Details: Central-West Orana Renewable Energy Zone (REZ)
The Central-West Orana REZ will be serviced by new transmission network infrastructure, including high-capacity transmission lines and energy hubs, which will transfer power generated by solar and wind farms to electricity consumers. This infrastructure will be critical for the successful operation of the REZ. EnergyCo is leading the development of the REZ transmission network infrastructure in its capacity as the Infrastructure Planner for the REZs in NSW, and is responsible for developing and overseeing the planning and approval processes for the REZ network infrastructure.
EnergyCo has selected ACE Energy as the first ranked proponent as Network Operator for the Central-West Orana REZ. ACE Energy is sponsored by Acciona, Cobra and Endeavour Energy.
As the network operator, ACE Energy will be responsible for designing, financing, building, operating and maintaining the network infrastructure for the clean energy zone. The infrastructure will include energy hubs and new high-capacity transmission lines.
Centred around the town of Dubbo, the Central-West Orana REZ was originally expected to deliver 3 GW of new solar, wind and storage capacity but that appears certain to be revised.
===

Tunnel works (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1360904
Agency: Acciona Construction Australia Pty Ltd
Location: NSW
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 31/12/2023, 10/03/2024, 03/04/2024, 19/05/2024, 25/08/2024, 30/06/2025 & 30/07/2025. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 17/04/2026.

Thuy Ho
Operations Manager
P: +61 2 9927 3100
E: tho@icnnsw.org.au
Details: Western Harbour Tunnel Stage 2
Overview
The Western Harbour Tunnel Project (WHT) includes 6.5 kilometres of dual three-lane tunnels, stretching from the Warringah Freeway in Cammeray, across Sydney Harbour, to the WestConnex interchange at Rozelle, alleviating congestion on the Anzac Bridge, Sydney Harbour Bridge and Sydney Harbour Tunnel.
The Tunnel will be delivered in two packages:
- Stage 1 (delivered by John Holland CPB Contractors) includes excavation and partial civil fit out of the southern section of the tunnel between Rozelle and Birchgrove.
- Stage 2 (this project) includes the construction of approximately 4.8 kilometres of tunnels from under Birchgrove, through to the Warringah Freeway near North Sydney and tunnelling under Sydney Harbour. Stage 2 also includes the remaining civil fit out of the Stage 1 section, surface works at the North Sydney end of the tunnel, and mechanical and electrical fit out for the entire 6.5km length of the tunnel.
ACCIONA Construction Australia Pty Ltd has been appointed by Transport for NSW to deliver Stage 2 of the WHT Project.
ACCIONA is proposing that roadheaders will be used to excavate the tunnels on the northern side of Sydney Harbour between North Sydney and Balls Head. Two tunnel boring machines (TBMs) will be used to excavate the remainder of the tunnels from Birchgrove on the southern side of the Harbour through to a cavern at Balls Head where they will meet the northern roadheader tunnels.
The Stage 2 tunnelling works are outline in Figure 1. The orange lines indicate the TBM tunnels and the caverns where the TBMs will be launched and retrieved. While the light blue lines indicate the roadheader tunnels. The dark blue lines indicate the extent of Stage 1 works. Figure 2 demonstrates the tunnel vertical alignment from south at Birchgrove to north at Warrigah Freeway.
Further information on the project is available at nswroads.work/whtportal with an interactive portal here to provide area specific information to help you navigate the project.
===

Tenders.Net #: 1602282

Lodgement details
Electronic lodgement using the eLodgement facility
Faxing to Darwin tenders office on +61 8 8999 1935
Details: NTG26-0001 - All of NT - Supply and Delivery of Haemodialysis services, Equipment and Consumables for a Period of 60 Months
The Department of Health is seeking a single entity to provide Haemodialysis services across the Northern Territory.
The requirement is for patient treatment in urban dialysis centres, remote communities in renal facilities and patient homes. The capability of providing Plasma Exchange (PE) services in the Darwin and Alice Springs region is also included in this tender.
Offers must include as a minimum but not limited to the following:
Supply, delivery, installation and commissioning of:
· haemodialysis machines (HD) with haemodiafiltration capability.
· Home Haemodialysis machines.
· Able to monitor effective dialysis dose, access flow and recirculation.
· water treatment plants (reverse osmosis (RO) units and all components of pre-filtration required for dialysis).
· Individual portable reverse osmosis filtration units.
· water chillers for ROs and portable ROs.
· Dialysis appropriate chairs.
· Bedside trolleys.
· Device integration with NT Health electronic patient record – Acacia.
Ongoing service provision including but not limited to:
· Equipment maintenance.
· Onsite Technical Support
· Technical backup.
· Spare parts.
· water testing to demonstrate safe water for dialysis.
· Dialysis technology.
· Supply of all consumables.
Katherine Dialysis Clinic, 122 Casuarina Street Katherine East NT 0850, and Alice Springs Dialysis Clinic , 33 Gap Road, Alice Springs NT 0870 are excluded from this tender. These services are currently fully outsourced to a private service provider
Price Range Estimated at $70M - $90M.

External Accounting Services

Tenders.Net #: 1602073
Agency: Desert Knowledge Australia
Location: NT
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 02:00 PM ACST (UTC +09:30)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: Chris Kendrick
Email: chris.kendrick@dka.com.au
Mobile: 0427 397 701
Details: DKA are seeking accounting services including comprehensive financial management and reporting support for the organisation. This encompasses day-to-day bookkeeping functions such as processing approved supplier payments, reconciling accounts receivable, administration and payment of fortnightly staff payroll and quarterly Board member payroll (including superannuation, required ATO returns, and leave provision journals), performing bank reconciliations, and preparing and lodging IAS/BAS returns.
The services also include hosting and maintaining the Xero cloud-based accounting system, preparing quarterly management reports, reconciling Balance Sheet accounts, and maintaining the fixed asset register. In addition, the provider will offer financial advice to the Finance, audit and Risk management Committee (FARMC) and Board, prepare annual financial statements, supply information and liaise with external auditors during the audit process, and provide ad hoc accounting information or analysis as required to support organisational decision-making.

Lodgement details
Electronic lodgement using the eLodgement facility

Faxing to Darwin tenders office on +61 8 8999 1935
Details: RFT0577 - All Centres - Provision of Technology services for a Period of 60 Months
Power and water’s Technology landscape comprises of Information and Communications Technology (ICT) and Operational Technology (OT) environments (applications, systems, infrastructure, network, hardware). Power and water operates critical infrastructure and requires access to a broad range of specialist and generalist technology capabilities to support business operations, regulatory obligations, cybersecurity requirements, and long-term digital transformation objectives.
Power and water is seeking to engage suitably qualified and experienced technology service providers for strategic partnerships for multiple categories within Technology. Power and water aims to modify its approach to sourcing technology services from bespoke contracts for small bundles of work to enterprise-level strategic arrangements across ICT and OT environments.
This Request for Tender (RFT) is an open-market engagement, offering an opportunity to small-to-large technology service providers to participate and provide best-in-class technology and commercial solutions to Power and water.
This Request for Tender (RFT) covers five categories across ICT and OT services. The anticipated spend across the categories depends on the supplier capabilities and the final scope awarded to the suppliers. The tentative breakdown of the anticipated spend is as follows:
Category A
Application maintenance and Support (AMS)
$5 – 15m
Suppliers’ capabilities will determine the applications, projects and licenses
Category B
Core Capability Program (CCP)
$10 – 20m (for 2 years)
Suppliers’ capabilities and delivery model for Oracle, Maximo and future projects will determine the actuals
Category C
Infrastructure and Database services (IDS)
$1 – 5m
Support for BAU work and project support will determine the actuals
Category D
Cybersecurity (CS)
$10 – 20m (for 2 years)
Contingent on final requirements for SP-2 capability uplift
Category E
OT & SCADA services and Equipment Supply (OT & SCADA)
$7 – 10m
Contingent on future projects pipeline
The numbers above are indicative based on the current spend, future projects pipeline and changing technology landscape at Power and water.

Structural improvements to posts and slab

Tenders.Net #: 1602271
Agency: Torres Strait Island Regional Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Financial Services Department
P: 07 4034 5700
E: procurement@tsirc.qld.gov.au
Details: TC26011 - Lot 181 Kubin Road, St Pauls Structural Improvements to Posts and Slab
REPLACE HANDRAILS TO STAIRS AND TOP LANDING WITH NEW HOT DIPPED GALVANISED HANDRAILS: Handrails to consist of top and bottom rails and R12 vertical BARS @ 125 mm Centres. Handrails shall be fully welded and hot dipped galvanised. Site bolting to connect larger sections. On site welding of galvanised sections is not permitted. Avoid paint finishes over galvanising. Handrails shall comply with NCC Volume Two. Handrails shall be securely fixed with appropriate galvanised coach screws and bolts. Install hardwood trimmer/blocking joist as required ensuring adequate fixings. Patch and paint all bare and disturbed surfaces affected by the handrails.
REPLACE TOP LANDING AND SUBFRAME WITH NEW: (1.2m x 1.2m) Remove and dispose of existing top landing and subframe. Fix bearers to stumps, and appropriately fix joists to bearers at 450mm MAX. spacings. Lay 90 x 19mm hardwood decking to joists using 50mm galvanised fixings. Deck must comply with all current and relevant Australian Standards and Building Codes.
REPLACE BOTTOM CONCRETE LANDING WITH NEW: Demolish and remove existing bottom concrete landing. Concrete landing to be 1300mm wide x 1300mm long broomed concrete and to include: Clearing of grass as necessary to complete the work. Excavation of footings/thickenings and top soil (remove a minimum depth of 100mm of soil).Supply and placement of formwork, compacted fill, water proof membrane, footing and slab reinforcing (F72) Mesh, bar chairs, steel dowels to join to existing slabs (600mm centres), and perimeter bar (N12).Concrete and any other items necessary to complete the work. Concrete is to be steel trowelled and broom finished. Finished concrete to ensure surface water drainage from the building. Ensuring a smooth transition from existing concrete. Concrete shall be 25MPa. (U.N.O)All trade related waste to be removed upon completion of works, all disturbed surfaces to be levelled and raked to natural contours of the land.

Details: PSA00260 - Supply of Bulk Fuel, Oil, Lubricants and Associated Products
The services to be provided under this Contract include, but are not limited to, the following workstreams:
Workstream - 1 Bulk and Packaged fuels and associated products
• Supply and delivery of bulk diesel, bulk unleaded petroleum, bulk kerosene, and associated fuel products to various Council depots and storage facilities within the North Queensland region.
• Provision for Council-owned bulk fuel tankers to collect fuel, as required, directly from the Supplier’s fuel terminal(s).
• Capability to provide contingency fuel supply when the Council’s mobile fuel truck is unavailable, including the use of transportable mobile fuel pods that can be deployed to various work sites, along with the ability to assist with the refuelling of mobile and stationary plant and equipment
• Fuel management Systems (If applicable)
Workstream -2 Bulk and Packaged Oils and Lubricants and associated products
• Multiple suppliers may be appointed under this workstream
• Supply and delivery of bulk oils, packaged oils, lubricants, and associated products to various Council depots and storage facilities.

Tenders.Net #: 1602263
Agency: Torres Strait Island Regional Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Financial Services Department
P: 07 4034 5700
E: procurement@tsirc.qld.gov.au
Details: TC26012 - Lot 173 Tamwoy Street, Badu Kitchen & Bathroom Upgrade
Partial Kitchen Upgrade
REPLACE KITCHEN CUPBOARDS: Disconnect all associated plumbing and electrical services and remove and dispose of the existing kitchen cupboards to the residence. Supply and install new kitchen cupboards and reuse benchtops. Provide a minimum of one bank of 4 Drawers, each drawer to have a minimum 100mm high drawer sides. Drawers are to be Blum Metabox or approved equivalent, including a Cutlery tray to top drawer. Provide a child deterrent cupboard (minimum 300mm wide) to be included in the bench cupboards. Doors, drawers and seen ends shall be 16mm thick colour board with 2mm PVC edging same colour as door material (Colour - TBA). Include 96mm chrome bow handles. Kitchen Cupboard carcass material to be constructed from 16mm ply laminated white, 1 mm white edging. Carcass is to be securely fixed. Hinges are to be Blum 107 degree hinges or approved equal. Cupboards are to be supported on either standard (complete ply laminated fronts) kicker or a 150mm high SS legs and frame with a removable laminated ply kick front. Sink and tapwear to be reused. Reconnection of water services including any relocation repairs as necessary and connect drainage to existing waste. Ceramic tile splashback to be remained. Overheads cupboards to be remained. Reconnect all associated electrical services. Bench tops to be commercial cleaned. All works to be sealed with appropriate sealants. Paint touch up all disturbed areas.
SUPPLY AND INSTALL NEW PANTRY: Pantry to be constructed of 16mm white laminated ply carcus with 1mm white edging with 2 x 16mm Thick colour board doors with 2mm PVC edging same colour as door material. Unit to be 2100mm High and contain 5 Shelves. Pantry is to be installed in the location as shown on the Kitchen Plan.

---
• Contractor is required to program works within the property to ensure minimal disruption to normal tenant requirements and is to maintain limited level of access to the facilities (toilet, shower etc) where possible during the course of the works.
• In circumstances where the facilities or services (toilet, shower, water etc) cannot be used by the tenants and / or where the tenants cannot access alternative facilities, then the supply, connection, servicing, disconnection and removal of temporary facilities and services will be required to be provided.
• Provision of temporary facilities and services may in part or as a whole be requested to be supplied by the contractor or others and will be determined prior to works commencing.
• As this work may be conducted while the property is occupied, then the Contractor will be responsible to ensure the health and safety of all persons and property (including tenants, personal property and the property in general) during the course of the works. The contractor is to implement suitable processes to ensure the tenants, and others are not exposed to activities that carry any level of risk of personal harm or property damage during the course of the works.

Roof renewal programme

Tenders.Net #: 1602192
Agency: Fraser Coast Regional Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 14/05/2026 - 11:00 am
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Procurement Team
P: 1300 79 49 29
E: procurement@frasercoast.qld.gov.au

Mandatory Site Inspection
A Site Inspection is Mandatory for this tender. The details are outlined below:
Date: Tuesday 28 April 2026
Time: 10:30am at Hervey Bay Airport then progress to Maryborough (MSEP) and Moreton Street.
Location: Hervey Bay Airport - Don Adams Drive, Urangan QLD 4655; MSEP - 23349 Bruce Highway, Maryborough West QLD 4650; Moreton Street Depot - 47-51 Moreton Street, Maryborough QLD 4650
Booking Required: YES - email by 24 April 12:00pm to register.

Point of Contact for Booking: email - procurement@frasercoast.qld.gov.au

Other Details (incl. Dress Code / PPE): Airport Roof access requires suitable footwear, Hi-Viz Vest. Fall restraint harness also required should there b a requirement to go within 2m of the roof edge. Other sites are only able to be inspected from ground level.

Inspection at Hervey Bay Airport will require Tenderers to bring Driver's Licence or Passport to sign in and receive a visitors pass.

Submission of Tenders
Please be advised that tender response forms are to be submitted as three files only and in the following format:
- Non-Price Response Form: PDF including all supporting documentation as one file. This PDF must not contain any price related criteria.
- Price Response Form: Excel
- Contract Amendments and Particulars: Excel

Failure to comply with this requirement may result in a non-conforming offer.
Details: CTOS036 - 25/26 (CTOS0362526) - property Works and services Roof Renewal Programme 25/26 Financial Year
Fraser Coast Regional Council is inviting offers from suitably qualified and experienced Tenderers for Contract CTOS036 – 25/26 - property Works and services Roof Renewal Programme.
This invitation to Tender has three separable portions which is facility based being:
• Separable Portion 1 the roof renewal of the Maryborough Showgrounds and Equestrian Park (Arena and S Stables roofs); and
• Separable Portion 2 the roof renewal of the Hervey Bay Airport.
• Separable Portion 3 the roof renewal at Moreton Street Depot (workshop building).
The Principal may only award any portion depending on funding availability. Provide a discount if awarded two (2) or all three (3) separable portions in one process.
The Principal may award part or whole of the contract to a single Tenderer or to a panel of Tenderers.

Performers, market stallholders, workshop facilitators & food vendors

Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Please contact (07) 4041 6152 or email events@umiarts.com.au if you have any questions.

EOI applications and information available via the festival participants portal.
Details: EOI - UMI Arts Big Talk One Fire Cultural Festival 2026
Celebrating decades of First Nations culture
Celebrating almost two decades of First Nations culture, UMI Arts Big Talk, One Fire Festival will return to Munro Martin Parklands on October 24.
The free event is expected to bring together more than 3000 people for a family friendly celebration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander arts, culture, music and storytelling.
UMI Arts artistic director Lisa Michl Komanggén said Big Talk One Fire was shaped by the people who take part, creating a space where culture is both celebrated and experienced.
Expressions of interest are open for First Nations contributors across the program, including stage performers, market stallholders, workshop facilitators and food vendors.
Guided by the festival’s philosophy, Big Talk: real talking, real listening; One Fire: coming together with the spirit of community, the event continues to provide a colourful, welcoming and inclusive platform where culture is shared, stories are told, and connections are made across generations.

Compressor, blower, & pressure vessel maintenance

Tenders.Net #: 1602108
Agency: Central Highlands Regional Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement@chrc.qld.gov.au
Details: RFT250127 - Compressor & Blower maintenance
This RFT is for the provision of an accredited and competent contractor to perform the annual compliance inspections for the Central Highlands Regional Council water Utilities Department for Blowers, Compressors and Pressure Vessels, and undertake minor and major maintenance as required to ensure the equipment remains operational and compliant with relevant standards and regulations.
The works will comprise:
a. Application for Permit to Work (2) weeks in advance of undertaking works
b. Annual Compressor, Blower and Pressure Vessel Inspection
c. Provide service report, defects report and site photo’s
d. Undertake minor repairs or parts replacement while on site
e. Provide a quotation to return to site at an alternative time to undertake major defects rectification works. If quotation is accepted, complete the rectification works as outlined in the quotation to make the installation compliant with standards and regulations.
The Technical Specification outlines the works required, the location and number of assets.
Suppliers may select to offer services for all or a combination of separable portions of work consisting of:
a) Separable Portion 1 - Compressors
b) Separable Portion 2 - Blowers
c) Separable Portion 3 - Pressure Vessels

Details: 2026LAQ022 - Lockyer Electorate Office Relocation
The Queensland Parliamentary Service is calling for tenders to carry out a fit-out for the Lockyer Electorate Office, located at 15 William Street, Gatton, and requires the certified design and construction of the electorate office.
The fit-out includes and is not limited to certified design, demolition and construction of internal partition walls, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, painting, joinery, flooring, glazing, access control & security hardware, final finishes, auto-door installation and relocation of the office furniture, equipment & items.
This is a time critical project due to current lease expiry date of 31 August 2026. The functional fit-out must be completed on or before 26 August 2026, the relocation completed by 28 August 2026 and the make-good of the former office by 31 August 2026.

Gated parking Proof of Concept

Tenders.Net #: 1599554
Agency: Dept of Transport and Main Roads - NSO Technology
Location: QLD
Closing: 05/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 28/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Supplier query cut-off Saturday 25 April 2026 02:00 PM (E. Australia Standard Time)
Details: TL26-00294. Gated Parking proof of Concept (PoC) for public transport park and ride sites opportunity.
This proof of Concept (PoC) has been initiated to evaluate proposed technical solutions and the associated costs for deploying a gated parking system at Park and Ride facilities. These facilities currently operate with open access and serve both public transport users and general parking customers. However, this unrestricted model has led to a recurring issue; public transport users often struggle to find available parking spaces, especially at high-demand locations such as those located near hospitals and shopping centres.
To address this, the proposed solution will explore gated access that enables free parking for users who have recently tapped a valid go card or credit card on a public transport trip. This approach ensures that genuine transit users are supported in their journey while maintaining access for non-public transport customers through the possibility of introducing a nominal parking fee. The goal is to improve parking availability for commuters, encourage public transport usage, and better manage overall demand at key Park and Ride locations.
This engagement is for a proof of concept as a service. The Customer does not intend to purchase any hardware or software and the proof of concept should be fully packaged and delivered by the Supplier. The key desired outcome from the PoC is lessons learned and recommendations report developed in collaboration with the Customer based on the outcomes of the proof of concept exercise.

Sunshine Coast Regional Council
P: (07) 5441 8287
E: contracts@sunshinecoast.qld.gov.au

A site information session will not be held for this Tender. Respondents are to carry out their own due diligence in relation to familiarising with the site.
Details: ITT2524 - Construction of Emu Mountain Pathway and Boardwalk, Peregian Beach
Sunshine Coast Council is seeking a suitably qualified contractor to construct a shared pathway along the north side of Emu Mtn Road, Peregian from Old Emu Mtn Rd to the David Low Way.
The proposed section of shared pathway stretches approximately 900 linear metres and comprises of a concrete pathway and a boardwalk. The scope of work includes, but is not limited to:
• Concrete pathway construction;
• Elevated boardwalk construction;
• Stormwater construction;
• Signage and line marking;
• Guardrail supply and installation;
• Supply and Installation of precast islands;
• Public utility alterations;
• Working with TMR in a TMR corridor in accordance with TMR approvals;
• Environmental management;
• Cultural Heritage management;
• WH&S management;
Council will appoint a Certifying Engineer in accordance with the requirements of the TMR conditional approval and a superintendent to assess and certify claims, EOT’s and variations.
As required by TMR’s prequalification system guidelines, all works within the State controlled Road (SCR) boundaries valued in excess of $300,000 shall be carried out by a Local Authority or a Contractor, prequalified under the National Prequalification System.
For this project contractors are required to hold a minimum TMR prequalification of R2 B2 F10.

Details: Regional Arts Development Fund
The Regional Arts Development Fund (RADF) is a collaborative initiative between the Queensland Government, through Arts Queensland, and Gladstone Regional Council. RADF is dedicated to promoting the vital role of arts, culture, and heritage in fostering diverse, inclusive communities and strengthening regions.
The RADF objectives are to support arts and cultural activities that increase access to experiences in regional Queensland, grow employment and capacity-building opportunities for artists, and deliver on local priorities while promoting the value of arts, culture, and heritage.
Funding Priorities
Gladstone Regional Council’s priorities for RADF are as follows:
Creative Life - An active, vibrant arts and cultural sector with opportunities for all community members to participate according to their needs, interests and abilities
Unified, Connected Community - A cohesive community that values and celebrates its way of life, civic occasions and achievements
Animated Places and Spaces - Engaging and appealing public spaces and access to quality arts and cultural facilities
Valued History and Heritage - A strong sense of history through the preservation, promotion and visibility of the region’s rich natural and cultural heritage
Partnership and Collaboration - Productive partnerships and alliances within and external to the Region
Cultural Citizenship - Sound community-based leadership of a well-coordinated arts and cultural sector
Cultural and Creative Enterprise - An arts and cultural sector that contributes to a dynamic, resilient local economy
Funding Rounds
The timeframes for each round is based on the financial year. There are two rounds per financial year. Significant for each application, is to adhere to the due dates as no amendments or late submissions will be accepted past the due date. It may take up to 8 weeks from the due date for the application to be approved.

Deepak Urmode
Supply Chain Officer
P:+61 07 3364 0670
E: deepak.urmode@icnqld.org.au
Details: Arrow Energy
Overview
Arrow Energy operates four gas fields in Queensland's Surat basin.
Surat Basin
Arrow Energy has operated in the Surat Basin for more than 15 years. It is now the site of the new Surat Gas Project (SGP).
The SGP is underpinned by a December 2017 deal with the Shell QGC-operated joint venture. This deal has allowed Arrow to take advantage of existing QGC-operated infrastructure to reduce our impacts on landholders and communities.
Following our Shareholders' investment announcement, Arrow sanctioned the first phase of the SGP in April 2020.
Phase one includes more than 600 wells to be drilled from 2020, focusing on an expansion between Arrow's current operational areas at Daandine and Tipton.
This initial phase included construction of Inlet Processing facilities (IPFs) at Shell-QGC operated David and Harry Field Compression Stations.
IPFs enable Arrow to supply gas from its operational fields under the gas sales agreement.
Arrow will continue to use its existing gas compression facilities at Daandine and Tipton, with upgrades to the Tipton Central Gas Processing facility.
Phase one also include upgrades and connection to water treatment facilities.
In addition to using Arrow's existing water treatment facilities which will be improved through upgrades to Arrow's Tipton and Daandine water Treatment Plants, Arrow will send water to the Shell-QGC operated Kenya water Treatment facility.
Two new brine storage ponds were constructed at the facility to support water treatment for beneficial use.
Phase one is now in the final stages of completion with Phase two scheduled to begin in 2024 pending final shareholder investment decision.
Phase two development will expand into new gas fields in the northern section of Arrow's tenure north-east of Miles.
Construction is scheduled to commence from late 2024, and will bring an additional 130TJ/day of gas to market over 27 years, and will include but is not limited to:
• Approximately 250 new gas wells around the Miles region including gathering network.
• Construction of a new Field Compression Station (FCS) and a Steel Export Pipeline.
• Construction of a Metering & Flare Package at the existing Shell-QGC operated McNulty FCS.
• Construction of a new Hybrid Power Station under an Independent Power Producer Agreement.
Subsequent phases
Arrow's future plans include:
• Additional wells and associated gathering to expand across Arrow's Surat tenure areas
• construction of another new Field Compression Station at Lynwood, south of Arrow's Tipton development
• construction of another Inlet Processing facility (IPFs) at the Shell-QGC operated Jammat FCS.
More information is available from www.arrowenergy.com.au
===

Details: 25ORSR007 - Mile End Sporting Complex – Stage 1 Outdoor Court Sponsorship Signage (DIT058577)
With the formal take back of control and day to day operations of the Mile End Sporting Complex (MESC), formally the SA Netball Centre, from May 2026 onwards, the Office for Recreation, Sport and Racing (ORSR) has identified an opportunity to commercialise the stadium under traditional methods.
As a part of the two-stage process, the construction of the outdoor courts will be facilitated within stage 1 and the completion of the new MESC in 2028 finalising stage 2.
The opportunity to create signage along the eastern perimeter of the outside courts, running along the boundary of the Australian Rail Track Corporation (ARTC) corridor.
The stage 1 MESC outdoor court signage will be offered as 19 stand-alone assets that may be purchased individually or bundled for greater share of voice.
Purpose
The Office for Recreation, Sport and Racing (the Agency) invites companies to submit proposals for the sale of advertising signage at the Mile End Sporting Complex.
The Contract Term comprises a one-year base Contract Term and a one year extension option at the discretion of the Principal.
This document provides an overview of the overall engagement, in addition, the DRAFT Agreement (ITS Part C) should be reviewed for further detail regarding requirements.

Alex Vojdani
Supply Chain Manager
P: +61 447 277 175
E: alex.vojdani@icnsa.org.au
Details: Meet the Supplier - Sustainability and Innovation
Overview
ICN SA is planning a 'Meet the Supplier' event for suppliers to pitch / showcase their innovative products or services to buyers.
The "Meet the Supplier - Innovation and Sustainability" event is designed for suppliers whose products, services or technologies can deliver sustainability benefits to South Australia's major projects.
Suppliers are invited to express interest if they offer innovative solutions in any of the following areas:
Electrification
Solutions that contribute to emissions reduction and support the transition to low-carbon operations, for example by providing equipment that uses electricity rather than petroleum-based fuels.
Examples could include earthmoving equipment, forklifts and back-up energy generation.
Alternative Fuels
Technologies that replace traditional petroleum-based fuels with low-carbon alternatives such as biofuels (e.g. biodiesel, bioethanol), hydrogen or battery storage systems.
These solutions can include mobility applications that use low-carbon fuels.
Other Innovative Sustainable Goods and services
Suppliers with technologies or services that offer sustainability benefits for infrastructure, mining, or energy projects not included in the categories above.
Event Format
Selected suppliers will be invited to deliver a 3-minute pitch showcasing how their offering contributes to sustainability in major projects.
Proposed event date: Tuesday 25 November 2025, between 9:15 to 11:30
Major project buyers will receive targeted invitations. If additional spaces are available, invitations will be sent to ICN SA's general mailing list.

Details: Copper SA - Smelter & Refinery Expansion (SRE)
Overview
At present studies are ongoing into an expansion of BHP's existing copper refining facilities at Olympic Dam to construct a two-stage smelter and associated refinery complex.
The proposed Smelter & Refinery Expansion (SRE) project will enable BHP to process significantly more concentrate from across its Copper SA operations. The scope will include copper concentrate import and blending facilities, additional smelting capacity, additional refining capacity and additional non-process infrastructure to support the expansion program.

Details: SECU-2025 - security System Preventative maintenance & Servicing
To maintain, service, and optimise the operational integrity of access control, intrusion detection, CCTV, and associated security systems across all nominated sites.
To proactively identify, report, and resolve potential failures or degradation, ensuring continuity of service and minimising operational risks.
To ensure all security infrastructure remains compliant with relevant regulations, standards, and Nature Parks’ environmental and safety requirements.
To deliver accurate documentation, reporting, and stakeholder communication that supports transparency, audit readiness, and informed decision-making.
To provide lifecycle planning through condition assessments, end-of-life recommendations, and future-ready upgrades to maintain long-term system reliability and efficiency.
To support Nature Parks’ operational needs by supplying trained personnel capable of technical expertise, responsive service, and collaborative planning.

Details: ARV-26-0107 - Provision of Mountain Bike services - Lake Mountain Alpine Resort
ARV is seeking proposals from experienced, innovative operators to develop and implement a sustainable, high quality commercial offering for the resort’s mountain biking trail network during the summer (non-winter) season, nominally from November to April inclusive.
ARV is looking for a mountain biking service provider interested in assuming commercial operation of the Lake Mountain MTB trail network (including areas under licence outside the resort boundary) and associated activities such as:
- MTB Shuttle access services for the network
- MTB tours and clinics
- MTB events and promotions
This opportunity would require a supplier to assume management of the trail network, including the cost associated with any maintenance, upgrade and renewal, and in return would have exclusive rights to offer commercial services associated with the trail. Please note, this exclusivity covers commercial services only and does not preclude public independent access and usage of the trail.
This is a chance to play an important role in Lake Mountain’s green season visitor experience and realise the potential of an established product with strong uptake.
Operators are encouraged to consider a diverse range of activations, experiences, and value adding services that will enhance the current offering and create a vibrant summer destination for visitors.

Art gallery exhibit

Tenders.Net #: 1602090
Agency: Bass Coast Shire Council
Location: VIC
Closing: 29/05/2026 - midnight
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Visual Arts and Cultural Development Officer
E: visualart@basscoast.vic.gov.au
P: 0468 577 615

Applications from exhibitors of all abilities are welcome, if you would like an alternative way of applying, please contact the Visual Arts Officer.

The successful exhibition applications will be notified of the outcome of their proposal by email within six weeks of the application deadline.
Details: Exhibition EOI: Berninneit Art Gallery
Bass Coast Shire Council is accepting Expressions of Interest for the exciting opportunity to exhibit at Berninneit Art Gallery between 10 April to 29 May 2026. Berninneit Art Gallery is a purpose-built art gallery in the architecturally designed Berninneit cultural centre in Cowes, Phillip Island.
Applications are welcomed from artists, curators, collective and organisations operating in both Bass Coast Shire and beyond for a 6-week exhibition in 2027.
Gallery Vision
The Gallery is a professional visual arts space that brings the best in contemporary art to our region, and which supports and fosters local artists. Attracting residents and visitors alike, it is a curated gallery that is Bass Coast’s first purpose-built exhibition space. The gallery will present curated and touring contemporary exhibitions relevant to our community, and showcase works from Bass Coast artists, Council’s collections and the wider arts sector.
We strive to present well-executed and coherent exhibitions that are of the highest quality from emerging and established artists from Australia and beyond. The exhibition programme is designed to present works that support and develop the Arts in the region which in turn fosters a strong sense of local identity and encourages meaningful dialogue and participation from community.
The gallery will present a program of high-quality contemporary, regional and First Nations produced exhibitions. The gallery offers a welcoming space for community engagement, exploration and enjoyment of contemporary art and cultural events, where a multi-disciplinary approach to programming allows for new ideas, perspectives and forms in art and culture to cross-pollinate.
Please read the Exhibitors Information Pack: Expression of Interest for 2027, available via the link provided, to find out more about applying for an exhibition.

Energy terminal

Tenders.Net #: 1600048
Agency: Vopak Victoria Energy Terminal Pty Ltd
Location: VIC
Closing: 29/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 13/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 17/04/2026.

Vopak Procurement
P: +61 18 0018 6725
E: procurement@vopakvicenergy.com.au
Details: Vopak Victoria Energy Terminal
Vopak Victoria Energy Terminal Pty Ltd (Vopak) is proposing to develop the Victoria Energy Terminal (VET) which is a low-impact solution to ensure Victorians have gas for their homes and businesses for the foreseeable future. VET incorporates a floating Liquified Natural Gas (LNG) marine terminal located in Port Phillip Bay, 19 kilometres offshore from Avalon, as well as onshore facilities.
The Project involves several scope components including:
1. Marine terminal where the FSRU will be continuously moored and where LNG carriers will be temporarily moored alongside to transfer LNG.
2. Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU) vessel will receive LNG from the LNG import carriers delivered by ship-to-ship transfer. A regasification unit on the FSRU will convert the liquefied natural gas into vaporised natural gas.
3. Subsea High Voltage (HV) power cable connection which will provide the FSRU with power supply.
4. Buried subsea and onshore gas pipeline that allows natural gas to be transported to the onshore Gas Receiving Station (GRS). An underground shoreline crossing will be installed by Horizontal Directional Drilling (HDD) methodology.
5. Gas Receiving Station (GRS) which will receive the gas from the FSRU, provide custody transfer metering and treat the imported gas with additives such that the gas meets the requirements of the VTS for supply.
6. Onshore gas pipeline from the GRS to the connection point at the VTS
7. Onshore electrical power supply - HV transmission cables, terminal station, substation and any other grid connection requirements to provide electrical power to the FRSU and GRS.
Note: The FSRU and Onshore electrical Power Supply to the HDD entry point will be Build Own Operate (BOO) packages.
The project is planning for a Financial Investment Decision (FID) in Q4 2026.
Vopak is proposing to undertake a formal Expression of Interest (EOI) process to identify suitably qualified and experienced organisations to execute the Engineering, Procurement and Construction / Installation (EPCI) for the following potential Contract / package options:
1. Overall EPCI Contract (includes Offshore and Onshore).
2. Offshore only EPCI Contract.
3. Onshore only EPCI Contract.
4. Individual packages for Onshore or Offshore.
===
WORK PACKAGES
Work Package - EOI Closes
EPCI Contract(s)
EOI for EPCI Contract(s) - 29 Apr 2026
Individual Packages
EOI for Individual Packages - 29 Apr 2026

Offshore wind project (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1553023
Agency: Blue Mackerel North Pty Ltd
Location: VIC
Closing: 22/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 20/10/2025. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity timeline & details last updated on 17/04/2026.

ICN Victoria Gateway Support
P: +61 03 9864 6700
E: industry@icnvic.org.au
Details: Blue Mackerel Offshore Wind Project
Overview
Backed by two of the world's leading energy companies, Blue Mackerel is strongly positioned to be one of Australia's first offshore wind projects.
Located approximately 10km off the Gippsland coast between Seaspray and Woodside Beach, the project is targeting delivery by 2032. At up to 1GW, it could generate enough electricity to power up to 750,000 Victorian homes.
By delivering large-scale renewable energy, the project is set to generate meaningful employment, investment, and supply chain opportunities across the region.
Project Overview
Blue Mackerel is being developed by JERA Nex bp - a 50:50 joint venture between JERA Co. and bp.
With more than 15 years' global experience in offshore wind, JERA Nex bp is one of the world's largest offshore wind developers, owners and operators. The company's combined portfolio includes approximately 13GW of net potential generating capacity: around 1GW of installed capacity, 7.5GW in development, and 4.5GW from secured leases.
JERA Nex bp draws on the strength of two world-leading companies- JERA Nex's track record in delivering and operating offshore wind projects with bp's extensive development portfolio and offshore energy expertise.
The project is committed to partnering with the Gunaikurnai Land and waters Aboriginal Corporation, Traditional Owners, First Nations businesses, local communities, and Gippsland-based suppliers to ensure local participation and long-term benefits.
Blue Mackerel acknowledges the Gunaikurnai as the Traditional Custodians of Gippsland and pays respect to Elders past and present.
Timing: Blue Mackerel is currently in the early planning and feasibility phase, following the award of a feasibility licence (FL001) in April 2024 for a 163 km area within the Gippsland Declared Offshore Wind Zone. A 7-year feasibility period is now underway, during which Blue Mackerel will conduct environmental studies, planning assessments, community engagement and regulatory approvals before construction can begin.
Subject to approvals and final investment, construction would commence in 2029 with first power targeted for 2032.
Progress to Date
Already, Blue Mackerel has made significant progress:
First management Plan for offshore wind activities to be approved by the new Offshore Infrastructure Regulator
Geophysical and Geotechnical Marine Surveys (completed March 2025): These works gathered deep and shallow seabed data to inform foundation and cable design.
Wind and Wave Monitoring (deployed March 2025): A FLiDAR buoy and wave buoy are collecting long-term wind and metocean data.
Environmental Monitoring Campaign (commenced July 2025): Includes aerial marine mammal surveys and deployment of Passive Acoustic Monitoring (PAM) equipment to gather two years of environmental baseline data.
Opportunities for Suppliers
Our vision is to grow a strong Australian offshore wind supply chain. That means creating opportunities for Gippsland-based, Victorian and Australian businesses across various stages of the project.
The above studies will support early engineering studies, which will inform early design concepts, construction methods, risk management strategies, and detailed work package planning. The process of concept and preliminary design is designated as Pre-FEED (Pre- Front End Engineering Design). Pre-FEED works will support:
a. Key design decisions,
b. Risk assessment and mitigation,
c. Project scope break-down to work packages/lots ,
d. Approach to the construction execution and operations phase and
e. Precise cost estimates.
The ongoing Pre-FEED works are assisting the identification of work packages-lots ( ref. (c) above). The following main work packages have been identified to date:
1. Foundations fabrication
2. Foundations Transport and Installation (T&I)
3. Onshore Substation supply and construction
4. Onshore underground cables supply and installation
5. Landfall-Shore crossing (HDD-Horizontal directional drilling) for cables
6. Offshore Substation fabrication
7. Offshore Substation Transport and Installation (T&I)
8. Offshore Export cables supply
9. Offshore Inter-Array cables supply
10. Offshore Export and Inter Array Cables T&I
11. Wind Turbine Generators (WTGs) supply , T&I and maintenance
12. Operations and maintenance (O&M)
Engineering works to further break down (WBS -Work Breakdown Structure) the Blue Mackerel project scope and identify supply categories and work elements is still in progress.
===
WORK PACKAGES
Work Package - Full Scope Closes
Heavy Lifting and Transport equipment and services at Port in Victoria - 22 Apr 2026
Pre-assembly and inter-connection works of Wind turbine Towers at Port in Victoria - 22 Apr 2026.
