Connections services delivery partnership opportunities

Tenders.Net #: 1602369
Agency: Western Power
Location: WA
Closing: 04/05/2026 - 04:00 pm (AWST)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Step - Action - Details
1 - Complete the ROI questionnaire - Respond to all questions in the in the Customer Connection Services Delivery Partnership Opportunities - ROI Workbook. If you need to attach supporting documents, please email them to: ROI.Submissions@westernpower.com.au

2 - Register online - Submit your ROI via link provided

3 - Include the event title - Ensure your registration clearly references the event number Customer Connections Services Delivery Partnership Opportunities
Details: Connections services Delivery Partnership Opportunities
We're seeking Registrations of Interest (ROI) from suitably qualified and experienced organisations to partner in the delivery of Customer Connection services across the South West Interconnected System (SWIS).
The intent of this ROI is to identify capable delivery partners with the capacity, systems, and experience to manage customer connection works from allocation through to energisation within a fast paced, customer focused environment.
Scope of services
services may include, but are not limited to:
• Overhead construction services
• Underground construction services, including directional drilling
• Cable installation, including jointing, terminations, and connections
• Civil services such as trenching, excavation, and general construction activities
• Service location
• Installation of ground mounted equipment and substation equipment
• maintenance and replacement of street lighting across the network
• Other electrical services, including installation of consumer mains, pillars, conductors, and busbars
Partner capability requirements
Respondents should demonstrate the ability to:
• Deliver services across the entire SWIS
• Perform a mix of small point to point works through to large, complex projects
• Operate within a high volume, fast paced delivery environment
• Manage projects end to end from work allocation through to energisation
• Coordinate schedules effectively with Western Power and other stakeholders
• Manage materials, logistics, and supply requirements
Commercial and operational expectations
Organisations responding to this ROI must be able to demonstrate:
• Experience in delivery of the specified Scope of services
• Strong customer relationship management capability
• Robust KPI management, including performance monitoring and achievement
• Accurate project and performance reporting
• Detailed, transparent project invoicing
• The ability to provide capacity status and forward looking forecasts to support coordinated planning and delivery with Western Power

Attendance at the briefing is a pre-requisite to submission and consideration of a bid.

Details: PROMSA26058 - Cottesloe Primary School Sports Courts
Programmed facility management Pty Ltd (PFM) on behalf of Department of Housing and Works (DHW) and Department of Education are seeking tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors to undertake Sports Courts construction at Cottesloe Primary School
The Work includes but is not limited to the works, include construction of a new outdoor basketball court, including sub-grade preparation and the placement and compaction of sub-base and base course layers, followed by asphalt pavement installation. The court will be graded to achieve appropriate levels and falls to ensure effective stormwater drainage. An acrylic surface finish will be applied, complete with full line marking for both basketball and tennis. Works also includes installation of a 3-metre-high fence on two sides forming an L-shaped configuration.
All works to be carried out during the school term if required with minimal disruption to school operations during school class hours.

Lithium-tantalum project (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1415953
Agency: Liontown Resources Limited

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 08/12/2023, 14/12/2023, 20/12/2023, 02/02/2024, 22/04/2024, 26/08/2024, 29/08/2024, 28/03/2025, 11/04/2025, 09/05/2025, 02/12/2025, 10/03/2026 & 10/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 17/04/2026.

Joelle Lim
P: +61 08 9365 7623
E: joelle.lim@icnwa.org.au
Details: Kathleen Valley Lithium-Tantalum Project
Liontown is an ASX100 company whose purpose is to find, develop and supply battery minerals to the electric vehicle and energy storage industries. Liontown are currently developing the Kathleen Valley Lithium-Tantalum Project (Kathleen Valley) which will include the construction and operation of a fully integrated, low-carbon mine in Western Australia. Kathleen Valley is located in the north-eastern Goldfields region, approximately 680km northeast of Perth and 60km north of the mining town, Leinster.
Liontown is committed to establishing a strong environmental, social and governance (ESG) foundation. ESG is central to our strategy, and we are determined to demonstrate sustainability leadership in the resources sector from the outset of planned operations. We believe that the success of our business is deeply intertwined with the well-being of our planet and society. We strive to create a positive impact through our operations, and we actively seek suppliers who share our commitment to our ESG principles.
The primary focus of Kathleen Valley will be the extraction of lithium with a secondary focus on tantalum. The Project operating phase has an expected mine life of over 20 years. Kathleen Valley remains on target to commence first production of concentrate by mid-2024.
Liontown remains committed to working with, and ensuring that, Australian industry, particularly the local communities is afforded full, fair and reasonable opportunity to participate in the Project.
===

Subsea tieback program (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1141366

NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 17/04/2026.

Hengameh Kiani
P: +61 08 9365 7543
E: hengameh.kiani@icnwa.org.au
Details: North West Shelf Subsea Tieback Program
The North West Shelf (NWS) subsea tieback (SSTB) program is focused on using existing infrastructure to develop reserves from existing and undeveloped NWS Project fields.
The NWS SSTB program will use the same types of equipment, methodology and approach to contracting to develop these fields that will connect into the NWS Project's existing offshore infrastructure.
The principal NWS Project oil and gas fields are located about 125 kilometres north-west of Karratha, Western Australia in water depths ranging between 80 and 131 metres, covering a total area of 3,134 square kilometres.
Current appraisal and development activities are focused on opportunities close to existing infrastructure.
Offshore, the North Rankin Complex, the Goodwyn A platform (GWA) and the Angel platform dehydrate the raw hydrocarbons for export via a dual subsea pipeline system to the Karratha Gas Plant (KGP).
The NWS Project's onshore KGP consists of five LNG trains and a domestic gas plant and is located on the Burrup Peninsula of North Western Australia, near the town of Karratha. The KGP processes raw dehydrated gas and exports LNG, condensate and LPG via ship together with conditioned gas via the domestic gas pipeline.
===

• Managing and maintaining Midrange UNIX infrastructure

• Performing incident response and problem management activities

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: tenders@horticulture.com.au
Details: Horticulture Innovation Australia (Hort Innovation) is a not-for-profit, grower-owned Research and Development Corporation (RDC) for Australia’s $17 billion horticulture industry. Hort Innovation invests around $170 million in research and development (R&D) and marketing programs annually to provide benefit to industry and the wider community.
Hort Innovation is seeking a suitably qualified, experienced and capable delivery partner for the project: Business case for avocado innovation model (AV25018).
The objectives of the services being sought are to:
• Comprehensive development and evaluation of an avocado innovation model/s responding to strategic industry challenges and opportunities within avocado production R&D
• Support effective industry investment through a clear, evidence-based business case and implementation plan
• Clearly communication to levy payers and other stakeholders the outcomes and benefits to industry of this investment
Hort Innovation has prepared a Request for Proposal (RFP) document that provides background information, outlines the scope of services, the procurement process and the information required from interested parties.
The RFP may be downloaded from www.tenders.net (you will need to be a member of tenders.net to access this information).
The closing date for responses is 19/05/2026 3:00 PM (AEST).

Details: Horticulture Innovation Australia (Hort Innovation) is a not-for-profit, grower-owned Research and Development Corporation (RDC) for Australia’s $17 billion horticulture industry. Hort Innovation invests around $170 million in research and development (R&D) and marketing programs annually to provide benefit to industry and the wider community.
Hort Innovation is seeking a suitably qualified, experienced and capable delivery partner for the project: On-farm Innovation Program for Rubus (RB25002).
The objectives of the services being sought are to:
• Design and deliver a scalable on-farm innovation program framework that enables rapid, on‑farm trials and demonstrations of AgTech solutions in the Rubus industry
• De‑risk and accelerate adoption of AgTech innovations by validating their technical performance and business value under real commercial farming conditions
• Build grower confidence and capability through practical demonstrations and peer‑to‑peer learning, supporting the adoption of solutions that improve productivity, sustainability, and long‑term industry viability
Hort Innovation has prepared a Request for Proposal (RFP) document that provides background information, outlines the scope of services, the procurement process and the information required from interested parties.

Details: Kiribati
---
RFx ID: 33929641 - Kiribati Health Sector Support Programme (Reference #: ACT-0102882)
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade is planning to procure one or more suppliers to design and implement an international development programme to improve the quality, coverage, and inclusivity of health services in Kiribati. MFAT is looking to contract the same supplier/s to deliver a design inception phase (including an option analysis) detailed design and implementation. There will be two stop/go clause’s one between the design inception phase and detailed design phase and the second between the detailed design phase and implementation.
The proposed Kiribati–New Zealand Health Sector Support Programme will be a strategic investment of approximately NZ$20 million over six years (2026–2032). The programme will have a primary focus on reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health (RMNCAH) and the prevention and management of non communicable diseases (NCDs).
See attachments via link provided for further information.

Details: AGO24/25-005 - GeoPanel Refresh: Category 3 and Sub-category 4B
The Commonwealth of Australia represented by the Department of Defence is seeking to extend its current capabilities for GeoPanel Category 3 - Data Production, Data Translation and Map Production. It is also seeking to extend current capabilities for GeoPanel Sub-category 4B by expanding Category 4B to incorporate Hydrographic, Oceanographic, Meteorological and Maritime GEOINT Data and Products.
Existing GeoPanel Contractors who are already approved in relation to either or both Category 3 Data Production, Data Translation and Map Production or Sub-category 4B Hydrographic, Oceanographic, Meteorological and Maritime GEOINT Data and Products do not need to provide a response to this RFT.
conditions for Participation:
The Commonwealth will exclude a response from further consideration if the Commonwealth considers that:
• For Category 3C only, the Tenderer has not submitted, by the Closing Time, sample data in accordance with the requirements of COT Annex C clause 3.4;
• The Tenderer does not exist as a legal entity at the Closing Time;
• The Tenderer is named as non-compliant with the Workplace Gender Equality Act 2012 (Cth) (WGE Act), where the Tenderer is a relevant employer for the purposes of the WGE Act (clause 1.7); and
• The Tenderer does not hold a valid and satisfactory Statement of Tax Record, or a tax receipt issued by the ATO at the Closing Time (refer to clause 1.11).

Invite only: Senior lead developer developer

Tenders.Net #: 1602304
Agency: Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry
Location: ALL
Closing: 27/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

E: DBDProcurement@aff.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06235 - 1 Senior Lead Developer Developer
Job details
The role will require that you work across both Biosecurity systems and Export systems to help drive a program of work to integrate platforms to a single unified portal starting with SSO and account, roles and privileges, building to a full legacy migration.
It will require working with multiple different technology stacks and navigating codebases ranging from Delphi and .VB to modern C# and Dynamics 365. Working with a small team that includes a Lead Architect/ Developer, Developers, Testers and Designers you will be working with product teams to help understand their systems and dependencies to help scope and drive system changes for relying parties, scope SSO effort and changes to attribute models that support delivery and update architectural documents to run through governance, cyber and oversight forums. You will take your steer from the product owner and lead developer to drive internal teams updating through dev-ops to scope work and ensure timelines are met.
Key duties and responsibilities
Key Duties and Responsibilities
Hands-on Engineering: Act as the primary contributor for complex coding tasks across C#, ASPX, SQL, and JScript, while providing maintenance and logic-extraction for legacy Delphi, VB, and ASP systems.
System Architecture: Define the technical blueprint for how our custom applications, databases, and Dynamics 365 ecosystem communicate, ensuring long-term stability and performance.
Technical Project management: Bridge the gap between the "what" and the "how." You will oversee project timelines, manage technical risks, and ensure that architectural goals align with delivery schedules.
Mentorship & Best Practices: You will set the standard for code quality, documentation (XML/HTML), and database schema design, mentoring the team through complex migrations and integrations.
Required Skills and Experience
• Demonstrable evidence of progressive, documented IT experience (circa 20 years), illustrating an experience trajectory from legacy client-server systems that evolved alongside the industry into modern cloud-based ERP and CRM environments.
• Demonstrated evidence showing a rich understanding of hands-on knowledge across a broad range of programming languages across modern tiers, legacy tiers and web & data.
• Demonstrated experience working on complex enterprise projects that required documenting architectural vision and applying whole of government standards, cyber best practices and legacy migration planning.
• Demonstrated experience in project management leveraging agile practices, benefits realisation, risk management, planning and documentation to support.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Wednesday, 29 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06225 - Minimum of 1 APS6 HR Payroll / Systems Analyst
Job details
Systems Analysts translate business requirements into technical requirements that align with relevant architectures.
This role is a Senior HR Payroll Administration Officer (DSS)
The role is responsible for the maintenance and support of the Department of Social services (DSS) ERP system including the HR Payroll Administration function.
The role operates within the Payroll Administration and HR Support team to ensure the correct operation of the Human Resources aspects of SAP, under limited direction, through the provision of system support, implementation of system configuration changes, performance and system testing as necessary, and updating of system documentation; and other tasks as directed.
The role is a specialist in, and responsible for, multiple components of the SAP Human Resources and Payroll modules and will provide specialist support for these components including the implementation of approved system changes
Key duties and responsibilities
Duties may include some or all of the following;
• Manage the function of Payroll Administration for the Department of Social services (DSS) and other client agencies
• Manage the fortnightly payroll process including quality assurance and associated mandatory reporting
• Provide specialist, third-tier support for SAP HR and Payroll, including first level system configuration
• Perform system testing and documentation
• Monitor and action queries on the SAP Help Desk including incident investigation and resolution
• Contribute to the day-to-day operations of the team including setting priorities, allocation of internal resources, and assisting with the development of work plans in relation to Payroll Administration.
In addition the following Knowledge / Qualifications are desirable
• Comprehensive understanding of the SAP HR Payroll Administration processes
• Comprehensive understanding of Superannuation processes and Interfaces utilised for Payroll reporting and submission
• Comprehensive understanding of Single Touch Payroll including reconciliation processes
• Ability to undertake baseline SAP configuration i.e. Superannuation Funds, Salary Sacrifice, Leave, Payroll Wage types
• Ability to interpret legislation and conditions of service within the Public Sector as it relates to the maintenance of employee data on SAP
• Ability to work as an effective team member providing support to the team leader establishing clear work plans and timeframes in relation to payroll activities, effectively contribute and also assist other team members to achieve quality outcomes.

E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06239 - 1 Release Train Engineer
Job details
Managing the release of new and updated services into production, ensuring alignment with business objectives and compliance standards.
Provision of Specified Personnel on a time and materials basis in the following role:
Release Train Engineer, to undertake tasks including:
• Establishment and management of the Platform
• Create/support Products that are used across Value Streams
• Manage/ operate the Platform services.
• Implement/use the architecture/methodologies standards.
• Respond to Value Stream requirements and capabilities that are needed.
• Ensure alignment with the broader Aged Care Transformation Program objectives.
Key duties and responsibilities
Scaled Agile Leadership Lead and continuously improve the operation of one or more Agile Release Trains (ARTs) within a SAFe framework. facilitate and coordinate PI Planning, Inspect & Adapt, System Demos, Scrum of Scrums, and other ART-level ceremonies. Act as a servant leader and coach to Product Managers, Product Owners, Scrum Masters, and delivery teams. Enterprise Delivery & Outcomes management Own end-to-end delivery oversight across complex, multi-team and interdependent initiatives. Actively manage delivery risks, dependencies, resourcing constraints, and capacity across teams and partners. Ensure delivery alignment to strategic priorities, committed outcomes, and benefits realisation. Stakeholder & Executive Engagement Serve as a trusted delivery advisor to senior leaders and key stakeholders. Provide clear, evidence-based reporting on delivery progress, risks, issues, and constraints. facilitate effective decision-making across business, technology, and governance forums. Governance, Assurance & Risk management Embed pragmatic delivery governance that supports compliance while enabling agility. Identify, escalate, and manage delivery risks and issues in a timely and transparent manner. Support assurance, audit, and funding processes through robust delivery controls and reporting. Continuous Improvement & Agile Maturity Coach teams and leaders to uplift agile, lean, and delivery maturity. Drive continuous improvement based on metrics, retrospectives, and Inspect & Adapt outcomes. Champion a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous learning

Invite only: Microsoft Fabric / Power BI Architecture review & optimisation

Tenders.Net #: 1602293
Agency: Australian Tax Practitioners Board
Location: ALL
Closing: 24/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Monday, 20 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: PCS-03803 - Provision of Microsoft Fabric / Power BI Architecture Review and Optimisation
Requirements
Purpose: (Full Statement of Requirement (SoR) attached via link provided)
The Tax Practitioners Board (TPB) seeks quotations for a targeted independent assessment of TPB’s Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse/OneLake) and Power BI environment to rapidly identify root causes of current performance, refresh reliability and access/permissions issues, validate the end‑to‑end architecture (from Lakehouse through semantic models to reports) against Microsoft best practice, and deliver practical, prioritised recommendations (including governance guardrails) and next‑phase actions.
The Statement of Requirement is intended to be delivered as twelve (12) business days of total effort, completed within an elapsed period of 10 business days from commencement (with flexibility), with any redesign/implementation, deeper investigation, detailed target‑state documentation and formal knowledge transfer handled as a follow‑on engagement.
Scope of services (Full scope attached via link provided)
This assessment will confirm objectives and priority focus areas, then review the current reporting and data platform to understand how it is performing and where key issues may be occurring.
The work will be time-boxed to the most material areas and provide a concise summary of findings and a prioritised set of practical recommendations and next steps aligned to good practice.
Deliverables (Full list of deliverables attached in the SoR)
Deliverables will include an executive-ready consolidated assessment report (PowerPoint) focused on Power BI optimisation—covering current-state observations, key performance/refresh drivers, relevant capacity considerations, and a prioritised recommendations roadmap—with only high-level Fabric architecture, security and governance observations where directly relevant.
Indicative timeframe
Refer to the statement of requirement for this detail.
Suppliers should propose a realistic schedule based on their approach.

Thursday, 30 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: PCS-03731 - Procurement of Data Specialist services for access and Home Support Division
Requirements
About the Project
The Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (the department) is seeking data specialist services to deliver on current Support at Home (SaH), Single Assessment System (SAS) and the Commonwealth Home Support Program (CHSP) reporting objectives. Duties may include, but are not limited to, the maintenance and evolution of an existing reporting suite, extraction of information from the Ageing and Aged Care Data Warehouse, remediation of any extraction issues, and developing bespoke and ongoing reporting for both internal and external stakeholders. Professional advice on reporting best practices is also highly desirable.
Purpose
The SaH, SAS and CHSP Data teams within the access and Home Support Division (AHSD) require data specialist resources to provide ongoing support with the maintenance of the current performance tracking and reporting operations, for the SaH, CHSP and assessment, including contributing to the new Aged Care Act 2024 wait times report. Furthermore, the implementation of SaH on 1 November 2025 has required the ongoing development of new reporting tools using new data sets.
Context
In view of the ongoing need for timely, high‐quality data on the SaH program, SAS, CHSP, ongoing support from data specialists is essential to ensure that outcomes are being met to a high standard.
Key Dates
ACTIVITY - DATE
Anticipated start date - 1 July 2026
Contract term - The term is until 30 June 2027
Contract extension option - 2 x 12 month extension options

Details: LH-05510 - 3 DevOps Engineers
Job details
DevOps Engineers work closely with development teams to provide (and support) the environment needed to deliver the organisation’s services quickly and efficiently, using automation and self-service. The AEC is seeking to engage a candidate at APS 6 experience level.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Work effectively with the technical teams such as the development teams and DevOps teams,
• Establish and maintain Azure DevOps pipelines, repositories, and artefacts.
• Ensuring that cloud platforms are current in their toolsets, resilient, secure and automated wherever possible.
• In collaboration with other teams in the work area, investigate and resolve complex issues with the operation of an application
• Ensure deadlines are consistently met and that a high standard of technical and design documentation is provided
• Assisting with cloud first deployment pipeline development
• Perform additional duties or assume responsibility of functions as directed from time to time.
• Ensure deadlines are consistently met and that a high standard of technical and design documentation is provided.
• Creating and overseeing processes that manage the delivery pipeline from development through to production.
• Working with project and operational teams to orchestrate delivery pipelines across design, build, test, deploy and run.
• Implementing continuous improvements, strategy, and best practice development in the CI/CD environment.
• Perform additional duties or assume responsibility of functions as required

Details: LH-06242 - 1 Lead Data Scientist
Job details
The National VET Data and Register team within the Skills, Entities & Data Branch of DEWR’s Policy, Pathways and Inclusion Division is seeking to fill a Data Scientist position to provide support services to Australia's national register for vocational education and training (VET), training.gov.au (TGA)
Reporting to the Director, the Data Scientist position will continue the enhancement of the national register data on TGA. It will drive the transformation of training package development data into clear, actionable reports and dashboards that support efficient training package development and review with a focus on enhancing the quality of training package products.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Produce and review data reports
• Undertake advanced analysis of data
• Cleanse and transform data
• Report findings of data analysis
• Identify opportunities to improve analytical approaches, tools, and reporting products
• Share expertise and support capability uplift within the team

Details: LH-06240 - 1 Lead Data Analyst
Job details
The National VET Data and Register Section within the Skills, Entities & Data Branch of DEWR’s Policy, Pathways and Inclusion Division is seeking to fill a Business Analyst position to provide support services to Australia's national register for vocational education and training (VET), training.gov.au (TGA).
Reporting to the Director, the Business Analyst position will work with technical experts and external stakeholders to troubleshoot operational issues, identify opportunities for enhancements to the systems and contribute to release activities.
Key duties and responsibilities
Key responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
• Assist the team with user acceptance testing in the development and release cycles, including completion of user acceptance testing, coordination with stakeholders, defect triage, and validation of delivered functionality against business requirements.
• Provide day-to-day user support and advice to stakeholders around the system operation and functionality, including web service connection for third-party systems.
• Analyse and support product owners in the development of system enhancements for complex IT systems, including the text authoring and publishing system.
• Using Microsoft Azure DevOps to report, prioritise and track system issues and process.
• Build and maintain effective working relationships across stakeholder groups and delivery teams, developing a sound understanding of stakeholder needs to inform system enhancements and delivery outcomes.
• Assist in the creation and maintenance of system documentation.
• Share expertise and support capability uplift within the team.

Community Grants Program

Tenders.Net #: 1602272
Agency: Waterloo Wind Farm
Location: ALL
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Email enquiries to community@waterloowindfarm.com.au
Details: waterloo Wind Farm Community Grants Program
Community Grants Program is part of its commitment to make a positive contribution to the local Mid North community, waterloo Wind Farm provides community funding to support locally-based initiatives that deliver positive and sustainable differences to the local community.
The program provides up to $30,000 in funding each year for local initiatives that aim to benefit the community in the areas of diversity, sustainability and environment, health and wellbeing and community participation and pride.
Funding priorities:
To be considered for funding, projects should address at least one or more of these areas:
• Community Diversity: Support for projects that encourage communities to connect and be inclusive of gender, cultures, age groups, nationalities and abilities. This includes events that celebrate culture and traditions, projects that aim to improve multicultural infrastructure, provide support for disadvantaged groups, achieve gender equality, or initiatives that seek to address social isolation and improve access and opportunity for everyone.
• Sustainability and Environment: Support for projects that seek to build or enhance community sustainability or the local environment, in particular through the use of renewables, the reduction of waste or via protection of the environment. This includes solar panel installation, recycling at events, school education programs, community gardens and environmental preservation programs.
• Health and Wellbeing: Support for projects that seek to improve the health and wellbeing of the local community. This may include physical or mental health programs, initiatives that aim to improve sports participation and access, sporting facility upgrades and education programs.
• Community Participation and Pride: Support for projects that encourage greater community participation and/or seek to recognise and celebrate the unique attributes of the region. This includes community volunteering initiatives, playground / public space enhancements, community festivals and events and local heritage preservation.

Details: Axify Pty Ltd
(Administrators Appointed)
The Voluntary Administrators invite expressions of interest for the acquisition or recapitalisation of Axify Pty Ltd.
Axify is an Australian vertical Al company focused on hospitality, with a core product centred on Al-powered voice agents for reservations, guest enquiries and other critical guest-facing workflows. The business has established commercial traction and recurring revenue, with relevance to strategic acquirers in hospitality technology, voice AI, conversational AI, and financial investors.
A confidentiality agreement and an information memorandum will be made available to qualified parties.

Porepunkah Local Area Traffic Management Plan

Tenders.Net #: 1601740
Agency: Alpine Shire Council
Location: ALL
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 05:00 PM AEST (UTC +10:00)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: Alex Smith - Senior Strategic Planner
03 5755 0555
alexs@alpineshire.vic.gov.au
Details: The purpose of this project is to develop a Local Area Traffic management Plan (LATM) for Porepunkah that identifies, prioritises, and supports the delivery of safety and connectivity improvements across the township’s transport network.
The LATM will provide a coordinated, evidence-based framework to address identified safety risks and infrastructure gaps, with a particular focus on improving outcomes for vulnerable road users, including pedestrians and cyclists. It will build on existing strategic work and translate these findings into implementable, place-based solutions.
The LATM must be prepared in accordance with the Austroads Guide to Traffic management – Part 8: Local Street management and align with the Victorian Government’s Movement and Place Framework.
Scope of works include:
1. Project inception
2. Literature review & data collection
3. Site visit
4. Community engagement (pre-draft)
5. Risk assessment
6. Concept Development & Options Analysis
7. Draft LATM Report
8. Community Engagement (Draft Plan)
9. Final LATM

Professional services for sustainability

Tenders.Net #: 1602383
Agency: Auckland Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 14/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/
Details: RFx ID : 33935712 - REOI for ACG Technical services Supplier Selection T4 - Sustainability
Auckland Council Group (ACG) - Technical services Supplier Selection (Tranche 4) Category: Sustainability
Auckland Council Group regularly procures technical and professional services to deliver a range of capital and renewal projects. With the main panels approaching expiry, there is now an opportunity to consolidate, re-categorise, and standardise, through this procurement initiative, how we procure and manage technical services across the different departments across the Council Group.
Due to the large number of work categories, the procurement will be released in 4 tranches (by categories). By carrying out the procurement in tranches will allow the council to identify and onboard suppliers earlier for some work categories without the need to wait for all the market exercises under this initiative to be completed.
This REOI is part of Tranche 4, and REOIs will be released individually for each category under this Tranche. Please apply under the specific REOI/REOIs for the category/categories you are interested in submitting a response for.
Under this REOI, we are seeking to identify suitable suppliers who can self-deliver the provision of technical/professional services for Sustainability.
For more information, please refer to the REOI documents.

Details: RFx ID : 33936384 - Waitomo District Council Streetlight maintenance 2026-2029
This contract is streetlight maintenance around the Waitomo District.
Waitomo District Council require a suitably experienced construction contractor to carry out maintenance utilising NZS 3917:2013.
The contract works include, but are not limited to:
Routine and Reactive maintenance
Emergency Response
asset Renewal and Installations
Service Request management
Traffic management
Quality, Health and Safety and Environmental management
asset Data Capture and Reporting
Stakeholder Communication.

Airport resilience project (incl oil separator installation)

Tenders.Net #: 1602377
Agency: Westland District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 15/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/
Details: RFx ID : 33935398 - WDC Hokitika Airport Resilience Project - HARP
The Hokitika Airport Resilience Project (HARP) aims to increase the overall resilience of the airport by improving the airports’ ability to support regional airline aircraft at higher weights and passenger loadings in a wider range of weather conditions and natural hazard events and improve the ability of the region to respond more effectively to natural hazard events. In doing this, WDC hope to improve the safety and security of the Hokitika Airport, protect the airfield assets, and increase its long-term maintainability.
WDC are seeking to engage a Contractor to successfully deliver the required civil works, detailed in the design documents.
The successful contractor will need to be successfully deliver:
• The extension works for the Main 03-21 runway Starter Extensions, through the construction of a high-quality granular pavement and asphaltic concrete surfacing.
• The resealing works for the Main 03-21 runway, with the overlay of asphaltic concrete (AC) to the full runway length and width.
• The resealing works for the existing 12-30 runway, between the taxiway and 20 thresholds, using Grade 5 chipseal and sand seal treatment.
• The required apron extension works, through the construction of a high-quality granular pavement and asphaltic concrete surfacing and installation of a new oil separator.
• The required drainage works, which include bulk earthworks and shaping in the runway strip, installation of new drainage pipework, rehabilitation of existing sumps and various remedials.
• Resealing works for the Taxiway from the main runway to Apron.
In doing this, the contractor must also:
• Be willing to work collaboratively with ourselves and our design team to deliver a solution that meets the requirements of the Hokitika Airport.
• Be familiar with all the processes and procedures associated with working airside.
• Be able to mobilise to undertake the works from 1 September 2026.

Heritage Protection Fund

Tenders.Net #: 1602347
Agency: Mackenzie District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 18/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Mackenzie District Council Planning Team
P: (03) 685 9010
E: grants@mackenzie.govt.nz
Details: Heritage Protection Fund
Submissions welcomed for the Heritage Protection Fund
Council are currently taking submissions for the Heritage Protection Fund. This fund is available to assist and actively encourage property owners and members of the Mackenzie community to manage, maintain, preserve and enhance the heritage values of items or areas.
It provides a partial contribution towards the costs of a specific project related to a building, item or place.
The fund is allocated $5000 each financial year by Council which is then contestable by applicants.
Eligible for funding are:
Buildings
• Items or places listed on the Heritage Items Schedule of the District Plan
• Trees listed in the Protected Trees Schedule
• Archaeological sites and wahi tapu sites as identified by the New Zealand Historic Places Trust.
Projects eligible may be:
• Preservation/conservation projects
• Enhancement/management projects
• Research and education projects.
The fund does not apply to projects that will demolish or remove buildings or items, or add or extend buildings or items, nor any work that has been completed prior to the consideration of an application. However, Council does reserve a right to consider these types of projects for funding if appropriate circumstances exist.
At least two quotes must be supplied that provide a breakdown of the costs. The criteria and amount of funding available for different categories of heritage items is provided in the Policy, as well as a full breakdown of the information that needs to be attached with an application.
Council calls for applications to be made for the Heritage Protection Fund in March each year. The applications are presented at a Council Meeting. Information provided will be publicly available as it will be a part of the Council Agenda.

Details: RFx ID: 33935206 (Reference #: 12011)
The DCC is undertaking a renewals project at its Moana Pool Aquatics facility.
As part of this project, it has been identified that the existing HVAC & Heat Recovery (HR) system requires some refurbishment to ensure continued performance and provide a comfortable environment for the facilities users.
DCC is seeking an appropriately experienced contracting partner to deliver the upgrade works on a design and build (D&B) basis.
The proposed Main Hall & Dive Pool HR system will provide operational energy, cost and emissions reductions compared to current operations.
Continuity of service is essential to the Moana Pool.
• The associated works will need to be staged and closely coordinated with the pool staff and the facility Events Schedule.
• The pool will need to remain open during normal operational hours 5:30am to 9:30pm.
• This may require some temporary ventilation when individual HVAC systems are down.

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown

Details: RFx ID: 33930699 - District Wide security and Noise control services
Waikato District Council is seeking qualified and reputable suppliers capable of delivering security services and/or Noise control services across the district.
security services include:
• Monitoring: 24/7 security alarm monitoring including installation of permacom/sim cards (or similar) for monitoring if needed.
• Patrols and Lockups: nightly lock ups for public facilities and scheduled security patrols of specific council facilities (internal and external). These facilities and occurrences may change over the course of the contract.
Reactive
• Staff Escorts and Static Guards: staff escorts and the provision of static guards at council events or facilities when needed.
• Callouts and Patrols: reactive security alarm callouts and patrols and contacting of relevant services and council staff (facilities team) when alarm is triggered.
Noise control services include:
• Responding to noise control complaints during business hours, and after hours: During business hours, Waikato District manages noise control complaints via their own call center. Hamilton City Council are contracted to provide an after-hours call center for noise control complaints. 24 hours a day, using these call centers, noise control complaints are logged as service requests and dispatched to the security services contractor.

Details: RFx ID: 33933852 - REOI for ACG Technical services Supplier Selection T4 – Contaminated Land
Auckland Council Group (ACG) - Technical services Supplier Selection (Tranche 4) Category: Contaminated Land
Auckland Council Group regularly procures technical and professional services to deliver a range of capital and renewal projects. With the main panels approaching expiry, there is now an opportunity to consolidate, re-categorise, and standardise, through this procurement initiative, how we procure and manage technical services across the different departments across the Council Group.
Due to the large number of work categories, the procurement will be released in 4 tranches (by categories). By carrying out the procurement in tranches will allow the council to identify and onboard suppliers earlier for some work categories without the need to wait for all the market exercises under this initiative to be completed.
This REOI is part of Tranche 4, and REOIs will be released individually for each category under this Tranche. Please apply under the specific REOI/REOIs for the category/categories you are interested in submitting a response for.
Under this REOI, we are seeking to identify suitable suppliers who can self-deliver the provision of technical/professional services for Contaminated Land.
For more information, please refer to the REOI documents.

Notice: Museum exhibition fit out

Tenders.Net #: 1602078
Agency: Timaru District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: Not Stated
Documentation: Availability Unknown
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: This is not a Tender.

Timaru District Council
King George Place
PO Box 522
Timaru 7940
P: 03 687 7200
F: 03 687 7209
E: contract@timdc.govt.nz
Details: First walls go up on new Timaru museum
As the first concrete panels go up on Timaru’s new museum, councillors have been told that the old museum will need to close in mid-2026 to allow its staff to work on exhibitions for the new space.
Timaru District Council chief executive Nigel Trainor informed elected members during the project and procurement committee meeting on Tuesday, and raised concerns about whether the job could be done in time.
“We’re looking at closing the current museum mid-this year.
“The whole reason for that is our staff are required to actually do all the storytelling and the penning of the information that’s going into the new displays. And if we don’t get that done, then we won’t be opening the new one in time,” Trainor said.
He also raised concerns about their ability to get through the workload. “I think they are going to be struggling to get done.”
Councillor Stu Piddington said this was news to him, and to fellow councillor Graeme Wilson.
“We should be told of these things well in advance ... no-one throughout this project has mentioned that,” Piddington said.
Council consultant Paul Haggath, who was managing the $47.2 million Theatre Royal redevelopment and new museum build, gave a comprehensive update at the meeting.
“We are well into the construction now ... inside the theatre, which you can’t see from the street, we’ve now removed the auditorium floor excavated for the foundations for the new floor, and that concrete will be poured on Wednesday [this week],” Haggath said.
He said there were some design changes to the auditorium floor after excavations revealed the foundations were “bigger and beefier” than thought, so there was less underpinning work required.
Haggath said the community would be able to see the museum starting to take shape from this week, with the first visible part of the vertical build starting yesterday. “The foundations are now dug and the first wall panels ... will be stood up at the end of this week.”
He said there was also “lots of off-site activity” with structural steel and precast concrete panels for both parts of the project being fabricated.
“They will start to progressively arrive on the site and be installed over the coming weeks.”
He said the project was “pretty well sitting on programme”. “We have made the conscious decision just to resequence one or two elements for logistics purposes, but with no effect to the completions.”
Haggath also addressed the obvious emerging risk to the project posed by the ongoing global fuel crisis and its impact on supply chains.
“It’s not affecting us yet, but we are very cognisant of this as an emerging threat and looking at ways to mitigate it.”
He said they were looking to source items from alternative sources closer to home, and avoid potential shipping delays. However, one item they could not do much about were the lifts which had been ordered from Schindler Lifts, instead of Powerglide Elevators - as originally planned.
“The lifts are an element that are coming from the other side of the world. We can’t do a lot about that, but at least we have ordered them now to give them the best chance of arriving in time.”
He said the intention to keep the project as local as possible had helped.
“In terms of fuel, fuel costs, it’s not affecting us yet. If the Government does put any restriction or rations on fuel then we will need to revisit that and see what the effect of that is.”
Haggath said he hoped the council was not too far from getting the new theatre manager on board, to “help identify gaps or omissions” that they could have made. “Hopefully, there aren’t any.”
The council’s general manager regulatory, Paul Cooper, said the new theatre manager, Scott Gaddes, was due to arrive on May 18.
Speaking about the museum exhibition fitout, Haggath said the detailed design was now complete and various aspects of that would be put out to tender in the coming weeks. He said images of what was planned in the exhibition space would be shared soon.
He said the civil works at the site had been completed and signed off.

Tenders.Net #: 1600069
Agency: Taranaki Regional Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 22/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 07/05/2026. Please confirm with contact.

All communications, including questions and answers, must be submitted through the GETS Q&A function.

A site visit is scheduled as part of the tender process. Attendance details and all key dates are set out in the RFP document.

Tenderers are encouraged to carefully review the full RFP documentation and supporting information available on GETS before submitting a Proposal.

Responses must be submitted electronically via GETS. If suppliers experience issues submitting via GETS, they should contact the GETS Helpdesk in the first instance.

This is an open, single stage Request for Proposal (RFP) process. Proposals must be submitted using the Response Form provided and include a separate priced and non priced response (two envelope system), in accordance with the RFP requirements.
Details: RFx ID: 33867729 - TRC - Land management Building Demolition - 08-04-2026 (Reference #: 2025 - 2905)
Land management Building Demolition
Taranaki Regional Council (TRC) invites proposals from suitably experienced and qualified contractors to undertake the demolition of the Land management Building and associated structures at its Stratford site, located at 43–47 Cloten Road, Stratford.
This project forms the final stage of TRC’s wider Accommodation Project, following completion of a new office building and upgrades to existing facilities. The works are required to safely remove an ageing and no longer fit for purpose building that contains asbestos materials and structural deficiencies, and to prepare the site for future use.
The scope of works includes:
- Safe removal and disposal of identified asbestos containing materials (ACM)
- Full demolition of the existing building and associated infrastructure
- Disconnection and removal of services
- Site clearance, levelling, and making good
The site will remain fully operational throughout the works, with ongoing access required for staff, contractors, and members of the public. Respondents must demonstrate proven experience in asbestos demolition, live site works, and strong health, safety, environmental and neighbour impact management.
TRC is seeking a contractor who can deliver a well managed, staged demolition methodology, minimise disruption to operations and neighbouring properties, and demonstrate strong environmental performance including waste minimisation and recycling.

Select a supplier to deliver and manage a Data network that meets Council’s availability, performance, security, and reporting needs, and smoothly transitions from current services with minimal disruption. This RFP is being issued by Hamilton City Council on behalf of Hamilton City Council.
A managed Wide area network service that:
• Provides site connectivity using fit‑for‑purpose access (e.g., fibre/DIA, wireless, LTE/5G) with SD‑WAN‑ready or SD‑WAN‑managed overlay
• Delivers high availability and predictable performance for critical apps (voice, video, SaaS)

• Scalability
• Simple commercials throughout the term.

Deconstruction of wharf

Tenders.Net #: 1595262
Agency: Napier City Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 01/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 09/04/2026, 17/04/2026, 27/04/2026 & 28/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.
Details: RFx ID : 33701439 - NCC Discharge Wharf - Deconstruction (Reference # : C2812)
The Discharge Wharf is a public facility owned and managed by Napier City Council. The primary function was the mooring of fishing vessels during loading and unloading operations, as well as overnight berthing. The Wharf was also fully open to the public and used for leisure activities such as fishing.
A structural survey undertaken by consultants has identified significant deterioration and a partial local collapse of the deck. The facility was subsequently closed to the public by the Napier City Council (NCC)
This RFP relates to the engagement of a competent Supplier to provide the following Scope of services for the De-Construction of the Napier Discharge Wharf. See Section 5 for further details.
The Principal are seeking a solution that encapsulates everything described in the NZS3910:2013 draft contract and this RFP
The demolition will remove the entire Discharge Wharf including the concrete deck and timber piles. The timber piles shall be cut at their bases at either seabed or revetment level. Reinstating the site post-de-construction, including rock revetment works, and returning any retained materials (e.g., pontoon ramp and security fencing) to NCC
The security fences around the site perimeter are to be removed to provide site access and will be reinstated at completion. It is noted that the fence alignment may need be adjusted slightly to suit the post demolition arrangement. The Contractor shall agree the alignment with Council following removal of the Discharge Wharf.
The Jull Wharf pontoon and associated restraint piles to the east of the Discharge Wharf are to remain.
The access ramp and security gate for the pontoon will be removed as these fix to the Discharge
Wharf. The ramp and security gate shall be retained and provided to Council for future reuse.

Nikita Rodrigues

A copy of the solicitation will be sent to organisations that have replied to and are eligible to receive this advertisement.
Details: Multiple Destinations
---
RFP 26-0237 - Framework Agreements for security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services for the WBG offices
The World Bank Group (WBG) owns and leases office space and buildings in more than 175 locations worldwide which are grouped into seven regions as detailed below. The WBG is seeking the services of Physical security & Fire Life Safety Engineering services firm (or “firms”) to perform the work described in this Statement of Work (SoW). The work described in this SoW will be performed in WBG owned or leased spaces in the following regions:
1. Africa East (AFE)
2. Africa West (AFW)
3. East Asia and Pacific (EAP)
4. South Asia (SAR)
5. Europe and Central Asia (ECA)
6. Middle East and North Africa-Afghanistan & Pakistan (MNAAP)
7. Latin America and the Caribbeans (LCR)
8. Service Centre in Chennai, India & Sofia, Bulgaria
For purpose of selection of quantity surveying contractor(s) these regions will be grouped in 4 super regions as follows:
1. AFE and AFW
2. EAP, SAR, and GCSIR
3. ECA and MNAAP
4. LCR
Mandatory Criteria:
Only those Offerors with sufficient experience, the financial strength and stability and the demonstrable technical knowledge will qualify for further consideration. The following mandatory criteria will be applied:
1) The Firm must demonstrate minimum average annual gross revenues of USD 3M over the past three (3) years from services similar to the Statement of Work.
2) The Firm must be legally registered and authorised to provide security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services, have been in business for a minimum of five (5) years, and maintain an office or lawful operational presence in the regions for which you are submitting your proposals.
3) The Firm must demonstrate successful and continuous delivery of security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services over the past five (5) years, directly performed by the Firm.

Details: Federated States of Micronesia
---
RFQ26-10085 - Climate Finance Advisor, FSM Readiness Programme with the GCF
This consultancy aims to strengthen the capacity of the FSM NDA Office to identify, access, and effectively manage international climate finance. The consultant will provide strategic, technical, and advisory support to maximise FSM’s access to climate finance, align funding with national and sub-national priorities, and support the development and quality assurance of climate finance strategies and GCF project pipelines. The role includes building partnerships across donors, accredited entities, government, civil society, and the private sector; supporting readiness and capacity-building efforts; advising on financing options for FSM’s climate action needs; and ensuring that climate projects are technically sound, economically feasible, and aligned with country priorities.

Beneficiary Account No: 36019
Narration: Purchase of Government Bonds

Once allotment of stock has been made, the settlement for successful bids will be done through the FIJICLEAR by 2.00pm on Wednesday, 22 April 2026. For non-CSD participants, if the settlement amount for the accepted tender exceeds the face value, the successful non-CSD bidders are to settle the difference by 1.00pm on Tuesday, 21 April 2026. If, however, the settlement amount is lower than the Face Value, then any excess payment will be refunded on the issue date.
Details: Fiji
---
Fiji Infrastructure Bonds
The Reserve Bank of Fiji, on behalf of the Government of the Republic of Fiji, invites tenders for the Re-Opening Issuance of the 25-year Fiji Infrastructure Bond up to a limit of $80,000,000 as follows:
Date of Bond: 22 April 2026
ISIN: FJ1528897580
Tenor: 25 Years
Amount Floated: $80.0 million
Coupon Rate: 7.00%
Maturity: 04 Feb 2051
Interest Payment Dates : 04 August & 04 February in each year until maturity
Minimum (Non-competitive tender) - Non-competitive tenders will be accepted in multiples of $1,000 for a minimum amount of $1,000 (face value) up to a maximum amount of $100,000 (face value) per maturity.
Minimum (Competitive tender) - Competitive tenders will be accepted in multiples of $1,000 and the minimum tender will be $10,000 (face value) per maturity.

Medical information system replacement and migration

Tenders.Net #: 1602346
Agency: Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD)
Location: O/S
Closing: 18/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: yoann.untereiner@oecd.org
Details: No Countries Assigned
---
3444 - Medical Information System Replacement and Migration - Implementation Project
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is launching this Call for Tenders with the objective of integrating a modern occupational health information system for all staff capable of full data migration from the current system, supporting end-to-end clinical workflows, structured medical records, reporting and analytics, role-based access for medical / HR / IT users, and scalable document storage to enable a “paperless” operating model.
In this Call for Tenders the OECD is looking for one provider capable of delivering the following services:
• Medical information system to support the management of occupational health, staff medical monitoring, work-related accidents, psychological and physical consultations, and
preventive health programmes for OECD staff with a robust role-based access, full auditability.
• End-to-end migration, implementation and change management services to support the deployment of the medical information system.

Norfolk Island Regional Council is seeking Expressions of Interest (EOI) from individuals interested in potential opportunities within the Environment Team. We are interested in hearing from people who have particular interests, skills, qualifications and/or demonstrated experience in one or more of the following areas:
• Planning and Land Use
• Biodiversity and Natural assets
• Climate Resilience
• Waste services
• Public Health
This EOI process is intended to support workforce planning and may inform future recruitment activities. All submissions will be assessed in accordance with Council’s merit-based recruitment principles.

Business support system with virtual wallet functionality

Tenders.Net #: 1602321
Agency: Tetra Tech International Development
Location: O/S
Closing: 15/05/2026 - 02:00 pm (AEST - UTC+10:00)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown

AIFFP Support Unit
Procurement Support
E: aiffp.su.tender@tetratech.com

Last Queries Date: Friday 1 May 2026 2:00pm (AEST)
Details: Papua New Guinea
---
AM 13265 - Australian Infrastructure Financing facility for the Pacific (AIFFP) - Telikom BSS VW CSA services
The Australian Infrastructure Financing facility for the Pacific (AIFFP) is the Australian Government’s specialist infrastructure financing program within the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. The AIFFP is an AUD4.55 billion facility investing in transformational infrastructure across a range of sectors in the Pacific and Timor‑Leste, including maritime, airports, energy, telecommunications, road transport and urban infrastructure. Export Finance Australia acts as the lender of record. The AIFFP provides financing to deliver quality infrastructure with a focus on strong social and environmental safeguards, generating local jobs and responding to the priorities of diverse communities. The AIFFP builds on Australia’s longstanding partnerships with the Pacific and its experience delivering quality aid programming that supports sustained development outcomes for communities. The AIFFP began operating on 1 July 2019 and has 57 signed infrastructure projects across the Pacific and Timor-Leste.
The AIFFP is managed by the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade, with support from Tetra Tech (the Support Unit). The AIFFP Support Unit provides advice and services to support the delivery of the AIFFP and its objectives.
AIFFP is seeking to engage a client-side advisory (‘the Consultant’) to support PNG Telikom Limited (‘Telikom’) with the contracting, implementation and integration of a Business Support System (BSS) with Virtual Wallet functionality (VW) (‘the New System’). Telikom is the project proponent and has overall responsibility for project delivery.
Telikom is currently using two BSS systems. Telikom seeks to switch to a new, modern single BSS. Telikom envisages the new BSS will streamline billing processes, improve operational efficiency, and support long-term growth. It also wants to ensure a secure and trusted network that meets high-level industry standards during integration and ongoing operation. Telikom has conducted a procurement process for the New System and has identified a preferred supplier (‘the Supplier’).
Telikom and AIFFP have identified a need for Telikom to be supported by the Consultant through the process of finalising a contract with the Supplier (‘Supply Contract’) and technical implementation and integration of the New System into Telikom’s existing IT environment and infrastructure. The Supplier would hold overarching contractual responsibility for implementation and integration of the New System.
services will be required from May until end June 2026, with a possible extension until 30 June 2027. The full requirements are set out in the terms of reference at Part B.

Beneficiary tracer studies

Tenders.Net #: 1602212
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 28/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Augustine Mayabi
E: nyotanpm24@gmail.com
Details: Kenya
---
Project ID: P179414
Project Title: National Youth Opportunities Towards Advancement Project (NYOTA)
Notice No: OP00434339
Borrower Bid Reference: KE-SDYA-484742-CS-QCBS
Consultancy services for the Provision of Beneficiary Tracer Studies for Nyota Project
The consulting services (“the services”) include Beneficiary Tracer study and Impact Evaluation Firm(s)in partnership with the World Bank, is supporting Youth Employment in all the Counties of Kenya through the National Youth Opportunities Towards Advancement (NYOTA) Project. The project’s development objective is to increase employment, earnings and promote savings for targeted youth at a national scale. The Project will target youth aged 18-29 and 35 years for Persons with Disabilities (PWDs), with little or no education, who are unemployed, underemployed or in low-tier employment with very low earnings. There are 2.4 million youth aged 18-29 among the poorest 40 percent of Kenyans who fulfil one or more of these criteria. Such youth face exclusion from the labor market and are often unable to access government sponsored jobs programs. As such, the project will target approximately 35 percent of this pool. The NYOTA Project is expected to impact 820,000 vulnerable youth across all 47 counties of Kenya including refugees in the counties of Garissa and Turkana. The project is anchored on a solid premise that better employment outcomes are realised when there are more inclusive jobs with better earnings for disadvantaged groups of youth.
The information provided outlines the plan to conduct tracer studies on NYOTA Project beneficiaries in order to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions. These studies will be carried out six (6) months after each intervention is completed, with a focus on assessing outcomes such as skill enhancement, employment opportunities, savings habits, and access to government procurement. The aim is to track beneficiaries and evaluate the impact of the Project on their lives, including employment conditions, challenges faced, and overall success. The tracer studies will not only assess post-intervention outcomes but also analyse the factors contributing to success or failure, such as labor market conditions, socio-economic barriers, and the relevance of training and support interventions to actual employment and livelihood outcomes.
The consultant(s) will be responsible for administering the tracer surveys, developing a comprehensive methodology, work plan, and survey instruments, obtaining necessary permissions, and ensuring ethical considerations are met, including in refugee areas. They will oversee data collection, provide training to the field team, conduct pilot surveys, and compile a final tracer report incorporating feedback from stakeholders. The scope of work involves conducting extensive mixed-methods beneficiary tracer studies covering the entire project duration, tracing all beneficiaries of the interventions, and analysing outcomes by various subgroups.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

The Advocacy and Communications Section of UNICEF Venezuela contributes to fulfilling this mandate by disseminating and promoting the rights of children and adolescents in the country, raising awareness among diverse audiences to ensure these rights become a reality. To achieve this goal, UNICEF must continue demonstrating its leadership in positioning, branding, and defending children's rights, adhering to the ethical guidelines and quality standards that the organization applies globally.
To highlight the section's advocacy and promotion work for children's rights, it is necessary to produce point-of-purchase (POP) and textile materials that identify the UNICEF brand and the projects implemented with partner organizations.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Independent verification agent

Tenders.Net #: 1602196
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 28/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Farhod Allamuradov
E: allamuradov.farhod@gmail.com
Details: Uzbekistan
---
Project ID: P181956
Project Title: Project Preparation Assistance: Enhancing access to Tertiary Education through Student Loan Mechanism
Notice No: OP00438697
Borrower Bid Reference: UZ-MEF-11-CS-CQS
Independent Verification Agent (IVA)
The Program Development Objective is to improve equitable access, relevance, and sustainability of the student financing support mechanism in the tertiary education sector. The Ministry of Economy and Finance (MEF), through the Education Loan Financing Fund management Department (ELFFMD), is responsible for Program coordination and results monitoring. Key implementing partners include the Fund for Financing Education Loans (FFEL), the Ministry of Higher Education, Science and Innovation (MHESI), and participating commercial banks.
MEF now invites eligible consulting firms to express their interest in providing the services of an Independent Verification Agent (IVA).
The selected firm will be required to perform the following main tasks, as detailed in the Terms of Reference (TOR) to be provided to the shortlisted firm:
• Develop a unified verification methodology and inception package covering DLR 1.1 and DLR 2.1, including a sampling strategy and workplan.
• Verify the eligibility and correct categorisation of women student loan beneficiaries under DLR 1.1 through reconciliation of data from MEF, ELFFMD/FFEL, MHESI, participating commercial banks, and the Unified Social Protection Register (USPR), using an agreed representative sampling methodology for the academic year 2025/2026.
• Assess and confirm the functional operational readiness of the integrated digital platform for student loan tracking under DLR.

UNDP-MDV-00361 - Firm to develop project preparation facility/project preparation grant proposal
To scope and develop an investment proposal that both builds, refines and trials the mechanism of the PPF and leads to the submission for a preparatory grant to develop a more comprehensive programme on climate resilience and mitigation. Trust gap analysis

Tenders.Net #: 1602180
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement.mk@undp.org
Details: North Macedonia
---
UNDP-MKD-00798 / RFQ 798-2026 - Trust Gap Analysis
As part of the Smart Finance project, UNDP will conduct a Trust Gap Analysis, generating evidence on the current state of trust between citizens and local governments across the selected jurisdictions. The analysis will focus on transparency and accountability in local procurement, with special emphasis on gender differentiated trust dynamics, to inform regional programming, strengthen integrity systems, and support targeted policy recommendations. The analysis will be based on data collected in selected municipalities and will provide context-specific, indicative insights to support evidence-based policy dialogue at the local level.

Solar PV expansion, direct cooling block ice machine & electric boat

Tenders.Net #: 1602178
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Dwi Arisanty
E: dwi.arisanty@undp.org

Pre-bid conference will be conducted:
Date/Time : Monday, 20 April 2026 /10:00 AM (Jakarta, Indonesia)
Place : Zoom Online Meeting
Meeting ID : 812 5980 0097
Passcode : 075454
Meeting Link : https://undp.zoom.us/meeting/register/n16fNIHmQxKRbcWpV4csGQ
Details: Indonesia
---
UNDP-IDN-00829 - Provision of Solar PV Expansion, Direct Cooling Block Ice Machine, Electric Boat
The selected contractor shall be responsible for delivering end-to-end Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC), supply, delivery, installation, testing, commissioning, training, documentation, and after-sales services for the BRIGHT project in two locations (Lengora Pantai and Malaringgi Village, Southeast Sulawesi Province).
The expected output:
1. Solar PV Expansion and Direct Cooling Block Ice Machine.
Expand solar PV system (8 kWp) and install battery energy storage to power a small-scale, solar-powered block-ice facility with a minimum production capacity of 500 kg/24 hours. (Specifications of the existing Solar Power Plant and proposed installation locations are in Section 3).
2. Electric Boat with Battery Swapping System and Battery Charging Station.
Deploy one electric boat with swappable battery system (portable batteries) and battery charging station equipped with Internet of Things (IOT), connected to the existing Solar PV power plant (specifications of the existing Solar Power Plant and proposed installation locations are in Section 4).
3. Operation and maintenance Training and Module.
The battery-powered boat, charging station, Solar PV Monitoring system and direct cooling block-ice machine: provide on-site practical operation and maintenance (O&M) training with a duration of five (5) working days per system (set of equipment), as specified in Outputs 1 and 2, including system operation, basic maintenance, safety procedures, and system monitoring.
4. After-sales Support
The Contractor shall provide after-sales support for the e-boat, battery modules, and the battery charging station and direct cooling block-ice machine for a minimum period of one (1) year following final acceptance/commissioning, including warranty support, troubleshooting/technical assistance, and availability of spare parts and service response.

Development of national guidelines for climate resilient planning

Tenders.Net #: 1602175
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement.bt@undp.org
Details: Bhutan
---
UNDP-BTN-00148 - National Consulting firm for the development of National Guidelines
UNDP Bhutan is seeking proposals from eligible and interested National (Bhutanese) consulting firms for the National Consulting firm for the development of National Guidelines for Climate Resilient Planning and design for the Five Major Urban Infrastructure systems in Bhutan.
The assignment shall adopt a multi-sectoral, risk-based, and lifecycle-oriented approach, integrating climate change projections, disaster risk reduction (DRR), environmental sustainability, and institutional governance mechanisms. The level of detail shall be at design guideline level, not detailed engineering design, but sufficient to guide DPR preparation and approval. The Client will provide available datasets (hazard maps, climate projections, and sector reports). The Consultant shall validate data and address gaps using appropriate proxy datasets and internationally recognized sources.

Details: Fiji
---
Call for Expression of Interest – Office Cleaning services for the Delegation of the European Union to the Pacific
The Delegation of the European Union to the Pacific plans to conclude a framework service contract (4 years) for the provisions of cleaning services.
The contractor must provide the human and material resources necessary to ensure the professional cleaning of the Delegation premises in Suva, Fiji. The Delegation premises comprise a total space of 2,422 square meters, which includes reception, hall, corridors, offices, conference room, toilets, kitchens, doors, archives, storage, etc.
The launch of the procurement procedure will be at the end of April 2026.

Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Rachel Bagnall
E: bagnall@unhcr.org
Details: Hong Kong, Special Administrative Region of China
---
RFQ-26-011-RBAP-PSP - Request For Quotation for Communications services for Middle Value Donor Programme for the Private Sector Partnerships services of United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees Sub-Office
PSP Hong Kong is prioritizing its resources to grow the middle donor segment and accelerate income growth, while ensuring donors enjoy a meaningful and rewarding experience that strengthens engagement and retention. This momentum calls for a relationship driven approach strengthened by strategic donor development.
Middle Value Donor Programme development will focus on relational fundraising, strengthening direct mail and engagement eDMs, initiating a targeted annual campaign to upgrade monthly donors, and supporting upward donor movement and file migration.
By partnering with a qualified vendor, PSP Hong Kong aims to enhance stewardship and fundraising services for middle donors, optimize campaign performance, deepen donor relationships, and facilitate donor migration, ultimately fostering a stronger connection to the refugee cause.
The services contract is planned for an eleven‑month period, with commencement upon completion of the selection process, tentatively beginning in May 2026.

---
Project ID: P176776
Project Title: Cote d’Ivoire National Electricity Digitalization and access operation
Notice No: OP00438910
Borrower Bid Reference: CI-CI-ENERGIES-423177-CS-QCBS-JJA-24
Recruitment of a consulting firm to carry out a study of HTB line routes (225 kV line Man – Séguéla - Mankono - Korhogo)
The consulting services (“services”) include conducting the feasibility study/Preliminary Design Study (PDS), the detailed design study (DDS), and the technical specifications (TS) for the construction of new substations. The studies cover:
• The construction of the 225 kV substation in Séguéla and/or Mankono with two 225/90 kV 100 MVA transformers and a switching input on the 90 kV line;
• The construction of the Dikodougou substation with two 225/33 kV transformers;
• The expansion of the 225 kV substations in Man and Korhogo;
• The construction of the 225 kV Man - Séguéla - Mankono - Dikodougou - Korhogo line, estimated at approximately 370 km.
