Management of conservation agriculture practices

Tenders.Net #: 1602136
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 18/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: The original advertisement is in French and may not be translated correctly. Please refer to the link provided for further details.

Mohamed Laarabi
E: mohlaarabi@gmail.com
Details: Morocco
---
Project ID: P181587
Project Title: Transforming Agri-food Systems in Morocco
Notice No: OP00438913
Borrower Bid Reference: MA-DRA RSK-546682-CS-QBS
Mobilisation of an operator for the management of the project for the adoption of conservation agriculture practices by farmers in the Rabat-Salé-Kénitra Region “PAAC”.
The RSK Regional Directorate of Agriculture is seeking to recruit a private operator whose primary mission will be to implement the main activities of the project supporting the transition to conservation agriculture in the Rabat-Salé-Kenitra region. Their role will consist of:
Developing and managing the e-voucher system to ensure the traceability and monitoring of all actions carried out by the various project stakeholders (aggregator, aggregated farmers, and service providers);
facilitating the adoption of conservation agriculture practices by targeted farmers, from access to inputs to the marketing of their produce, while managing the incentives provided by the project;
To provide technical support to aggregated farmers throughout the production cycle (soil preparation, sowing, maintenance, harvesting, storage, and marketing);
To collaborate with service providers, particularly young people, so that they become strong and sustainable players in the conservation agriculture ecosystem.

Development of nutrition information module

Tenders.Net #: 1602135
Agency: UNICEF
Location: O/S
Closing: 01/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Judene Edwards
E: jam-procurement@unicef.org
Details: Jamaica
---
QRNutrition - Development of a Nutrition Information Module within Jamaica’s EMIS
The Joint SDG Programme “Empowering Jamaica’s Future: SDG Joint Programme on Digital Transformation for Education” aims to drive digital transformation in Jamaica’s education sector by integrating efforts across education, food systems, digital connectivity, and social protection. Under Output 2.3 of the Joint SDG Programme, a digital system will be implemented to collect and analyze real-time nutrition data at the school level.
The purpose of this assignment is to design, develop, and implement a comprehensive Nutrition Component within the Education management Information System (EMIS) to support the goals of the SDG Joint Programme on Digital Transformation for Education. This Nutrition module will enhance the capacity of the education system to monitor and respond to students’ nutritional needs and enable systematic, real-time collection, analysis, and use of school-level nutrition data to inform evidence-based decision-making, planning, and interventions.

Design & build of wastewater treatment plants

Tenders.Net #: 1602127
Agency: UNOPS
Location: O/S
Closing: 04/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For further details, please refer to the link provided.
Details: Kosovo
---
EOI/2026/62399 - Request for Expression of Interest (EOI) for Design and Build of Wastewater Treatment Plants at Thermal Power Plant Kosovo B, in Kosovo
This Request for Expression of Interest (EOI) of UNOPS intends to identify interested qualified and competent vendors for the scope listed below. Please note that this EOI does not constitute a tender process at this stage. UNOPS will issue a Request for Proposal (RFP) at a later stage for the purpose of identifying the most suitable company for the purpose of finalizing the project.
Development of the Design for the Construction of five (5) Wastewater Treatment Plants and one (1) Chemical Storage at Thermal Power Plant “Kosovo B” and
Undertake the Construction Works for the Building of five (5) Wastewater Treatment Plants and one (1) Chemical Storage facility at Kosovo B TPP.
The project will address the key components identified in the EU feasibility study with the construction of the wastewater treatment plants (WWTPs) and the chemical storage building to install a proper wastewater system and develop capacities of personnel to operationlize and maintain the plants.
At Kosovo B TPP, six different types of wastewater streams have been identified, each discharging at different points of the facility but are collected in a single channel discharging water into the river of Sitnica. These include:
Sludge produced by softening (decarbonisation) treatment;
Overflow of decarbonised and demineralised water basins;
Bottom ash removal;
Fly ash removal and disposal;
Civil sewage system including surface drainage;
Oily and heavy fuel oil water;
Following the implementation of a feasibility study, the development of a wastewater system(s) compliant with European Union (EU) Directives and Kosovo regulations was recommended. Building an appropriate wastewater system is to consist of specific treatment processes for each of the different wastewater streams, in order to ensure that the discharge into the river Sitnica is fully compliant with necessary environmental standards to comply with the most recent European and local legislation for wastewater emissions.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Agronomy, measurement & field goods for protected areas

Tenders.Net #: 1602116
Agency: UNOPS
Location: O/S
Closing: 05/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: The original advertisement is in Spanish and may not be translated correctly. Please refer link provided for further details.
Details: Cuba
---
RFQ/2026/62333 - Supply of agronomy, measurement and field goods for the National Center for Protected Areas (CNAP) in Cuba.
Scope
This request for quotation process includes the acquisition of agronomy, measurement, and field supplies to gather baseline information on the state of coastal marine ecosystems at the work sites and to assess blue carbon storage in mangroves and seagrass beds. This will allow for the definition of management measures for the effective management of MPAs and the implementation of sustainable fishing practices and viable socioeconomic alternatives for local communities, in order to reduce pressures on marine ecosystems.
01 Monofilament (Fishing Line)
02 Circle Hooks No. 16/0
03 water Tank
04 Measuring Tape 50 meters
05 Satin finish caliper 200 mm
06 Portable digital hypsometer
07 Salinometer
08 Hedge Trimmer
09 Machete
10 Non-Adhesive Marking Tape
11 Unisex waterproof Rubber Boots
12 Unisex Coveralls
13 Cap
14 Hiking Backpack
15 Insect Repellent
16 Sunscreen

Consultancy services in education

Tenders.Net #: 1602098
Agency: UNICEF
Location: O/S
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Adam Ali
E: aaali@unicef.org

John Philip Perez
E: jpperez@unicef.org
Details: Multiple destinations
---
RFPS-NYH-2026-503915 - Global Long-Term Arrangement for services (LTASs) for the provision of consultancy services to UNICEF in Education
UNICEF has now issued a Request for Proposals in order to select a service provider for the Establishment of Global Long-Term Arrangement for services (LTASs) for the provision of consultancy services to UNICEF in Education.
UNICEF requires specialized technical expertise from consultancy firms to support its education work, including analyses, programme design, research, and evaluations that advance results for children. To address increasing demand and ensure timely, high-quality support, UNICEF will establish non-exclusive Long-Term Arrangements for services (LTASs) with organizations in the education sector. These LTAs will enable rapid deployment of qualified experts to provide technical assistance, advisory services, and capacity building across headquarters, regional, and country offices, including Centers of Excellence in Nairobi, Bangkok, Amman, and Panama. The LTAs will accommodate diverse institutional capacities and pricing structures, with an emphasis on consortia that include programme country institutions to strengthen local expertise. The scope of services will be structured across ten defined service categories, which are as follows:
1. Education sector analysis
2. Education sector planning
3. Education sector budgeting, costing and financing
4. Education management information systems (EMIS) advisory services
5. Curricula, school leadership, teaching and learning
6. Digital learning
7. Education research
8. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning
9. Learning assessment and measurement of skills
10. Equity, Inclusion, and Out‑of‑School Children
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

---
026/RFP/SHS/Final evaluation of project 549RER3004 - Final Evaluation of the UNESCO Project "Developing In-House Consulting Capacities in Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, France, Greece and Latvia"
United Nations Educational, Science and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) hereby invite qualified companies to submit sealed tenders as follows:
Consulting services for the final evaluation of the UNESCO project "Developing In-House Consulting Capacities in Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, France, Greece and Latvia," funded by the European Union under the Technical Support Instrument (TSI). The evaluation will assess project results across six beneficiary Member States against the criteria of relevance, coherence, effectiveness, efficiency and sustainability. Expected outputs include an inception report, a draft evaluation report and a final evaluation report. Activities will include document review, semi-structured interviews with key stakeholders, field visits to a selection of beneficiary countries, and attendance at a horizontal project workshop at UNESCO Headquarters in Paris.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Donor support services for individual giving programme (IGP)

Tenders.Net #: 1602079
Agency: UNFPA
Location: O/S
Closing: 14/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: John Restrepo
E: restrepo@unfpa.org
Details: United States of America
---
UNFPA-USA-00010 - Donor Support services for the Individual Giving Programme (IGP)
UNFPA, the United Nations Population Fund, is the United Nations sexual and reproductive health agency. Our mission is to deliver a world where every pregnancy is wanted, every childbirth is safe, and every young persons potential is fulfilled. UNFPA works in more than 150 countries and territories, reaching hundreds of millions of women, girls, and young people.
UNFPAs Individual Giving Programme (IGP) is a strategic initiative designed to diversify the organizations funding base by cultivating direct relationships with individual donors. The programme focuses on building sustainable, long-term revenue streams through various giving channels, with a particular emphasis on recurring donations.
IGP operates through digital fundraising campaigns, direct response marketing, and donor stewardship activities to engage supporters worldwide. The programme utilizes Fundraise Up as its primary donation platform, which enables seamless online giving experiences for donors.
UNFPA hereby solicits a quotation for the following service: Provide comprehensive donor support services for UNFPAs IGP, encompassing both inbound and outbound activities, subject to UNFPA approval, donor consent/preferences, and agreed workflows.
● Provide timely, professional, and empathetic responses to all donor inquiries within established service level agreements.
● Protect donor trust and brand reputation through consistent tone and compliance.
● Support monthly donor retention through both proactive and reactive retention strategies.
○ Proactive retention may include early engagement touchpoints such as welcome calls to new monthly donors, follow-up outreach within the first 30–60 days of sign-up, and upgrade requests for emergencies.
○ Reactive retention includes structured retention efforts when a cancellation request is received, such as offering alternatives (e.g., amount adjustment, temporary pause) before processing the cancellation, in line with UNFPA-approved scripts and workflows.
● Maintain donor satisfaction rates of 90% or higher through quality interactions.
● Provide regular reporting on donor inquiry trends, volumes, reimbursement request rates, reactive retention rates, and resolution rates.
● Coordinate with UNFPA’s donor management team to escalate complex cases (high-value donor issues, legacy giving, legal threats, media attention, reputational risks).

Tenders.Net #: 1600759
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 21/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 16/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: Kenya
---
0002021730 - Legal and Tax Assessment to Support a Pan-African Pensions Infrastructure Investment Fund (PAPIF)
The objective of this assignment is to provide comprehensive and independent legal and tax advisory services to support the design; structuring; and operationalization of a Pan African Pension Infrastructure Investment Fund (PAPIF); ensuring it is legally sound; tax-efficient; and compliant across jurisdictions. The assignment shall integrate legal; tax; regulatory; and governance considerations into a coherent institutional framework capable of supporting a scalable; investment-grade; multi-country infrastructure investment platform. The Consultant shall ensure alignment between legal structure; governance arrangements; and capital framework to support a financially robust and resilient platform. The Consultant shall support the full lifecycle of the platform; from structuring through to establishment and first close.

Tax advisor for energy & power projects

Tenders.Net #: 1599605
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 22/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 16/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: Lebanon
---
0002021668 - Lebanon Gas to Power Project - Tax Consultant
The Government of Lebanon (“GoL”); through the Ministry of Energy and water (“MoEW”); aims to develop an integrated gas-to-power project in partnership with the private sector in Deir Ammar; in the North of Lebanon (the “Project”).
The International Finance Corporation (“IFC”) has been hired by MoEW to act as lead transaction advisor to assist in structuring and tendering the project.
The Project consists of three interrelated components to be delivered through one or more public-private partnership (“PPP”) transactions:
1. Deployment of a Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (“FSRU”) to import; store; and regasify liquefied natural gas (“LNG”); and LNG supply;
2. Life extension and transition of the existing 465 MW Deir Ammar I combined-cycle power plant to operate on natural gas; including its transfer to a private operator; and
3. Development of a greenfield Deir Ammar II combined-cycle gas turbine (CCGT) plant.
To support the Project; IFC intends to retain a tax advisor (the “Consultant”) with experience in advising on the Lebanese tax framework for energy and power projects. The Consultant will assist IFC in assessing the tax regime applicable to the project.

Common Issues + FAQs

What does 'Availability Unknown' Mean?

Tenders.Net does not know of any electronic documentation or electronic tender boxes for this opportunity at this time. Please refer the tender contact details instead.

If any such information is later released, we will send out the advertisement again with the [Updated] flag.


I Get 'Page Not Found' when I click on a link.

Please make sure you have an active connection to the internet and have not used up your data limit. If your connection is fine then the linked site may be temporarily down. We mark any link that goes outside of Tenders.Net with an asterisk ( * ).

If the link continues to fail you should then use the tender contact details instead for assistance.


I can't log in to the 'Documentation' or 'Lodgement' links.

If you are being taken to an external site - the link will be marked with an asterisk ( * ) - then your Tenders.Net login details will not work. You will need another access to that site.

If you are asked to connect to www.tenders.net and do not have an access please contact us or register to get a login. If you cannot remember your access you can retrieve your password here.


What is a 'Selective' or 'Invitation Only' Tender?

Unlike public tendering where anyone can make a response, some opportunities are restricted only to those who have been directly invited. These are sometimes the result of a public EOI.

You will not normally be able to respond to these opportunities unless you have been invited.

Empower your organisations procurement practices with Tenders.Net. By advertising your RFx opportunity on Tenders.Net you reach thousands of suppliers (like you!) through our dedicated notification system.

Designed to meet the requirements of Government, the system provides audit logs for your entire opportunity, dedicated cloud lodgement facilities, supplier forums and much more.
